content.text:
https://github.com/ossianhempel/things3-cli is a pretty well functioning project for things. i also have Projects/Timelines-Sync 

could you rewrite something like Timelines-Sync but for things, based on the approach of things3-cli

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- atlas-cli: Isak's sync/automation hub CLI (`atlas`): the Mac mini's always-on worker + HTTP API (iOS Shortcuts) and one-off sync jobs — Todoist expand/mirror/label-track, Garmin/Strava → Timelines, midnight split, snap. Use for running or debugging those sync jobs and `atlas doctor`. NOT for project management (that's `pm` — see the pm-cli skill); for single-system work use tt / td / ntn.
- chrome-cli: Control the running Google Chrome from the command line — list/open/close/reload tabs, read page source, run JavaScript in a tab.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- garmin-cli: Read Isak's Garmin Connect data (sleep, activities, daily vitals, account profile, strength-training sets) and write Connect+ nutrition Quick Add entries, via the `garmin` CLI. Use when the user asks about their sleep, workouts/activities, steps/HRV/body-battery/weight/VO2max, gym sets, or wants to log calories/macros to Garmin.
- notion-cli: Use the Notion CLI (`ntn`) to interact with the Notion API, manage workers, and upload files. Use when the user asks to "call the Notion API", "deploy a worker", "upload a file to Notion", "create a page", "query a database", or any task involving the `ntn` command.
- pm-cli: Isak's project-manager CLI (`pm`) — cross-system project management over Notion (source of truth), Todoist, Timelines, GitHub, and local checkouts: create (`pm new`) / clone / status / list projects, pull/push every local repo, and check or repair cross-system drift (`pm sync [--fix]`). Also documents the manual move/merge/remove-a-project workflow. Use for anything project-tree related.
- pr-bulletproof: Harden the PR in the current worktree against every pedantic reviewer nit before anyone sees it. Runs a normal correctness review plus an adversarial pedantic pass (i18n, a11y, dead code, naming, React hygiene, style-rule violations), shows you the full findings list with a fix/keep call on each, then applies the fixes and verifies with typecheck + lint. Use when you want a PR to survive a hostile review with zero comments, or the user says 'make this bulletproof' / 'fix anything a commenter could raise'.
- pr-review: Review the PR in the current worktree as a thoughtful human reviewer and produce a ready-to-post review: a plain-English explanation of what changed and how, concrete things to verify (visual checks, tests, data/migration checks, or whatever fits), suggested inline comments anchored to file:line, a main review comment, and a clear approve / request-changes / comment verdict with reasoning. Use when the user says 'review this PR', 'should I approve this', 'what should I check before merging', or wants a reviewer's take rather than an automated bug hunt or auto-fix.
- timetrack-cli: Read and manage Isak's time tracking (events, timelines, the running timer — start/stop/add/edit/delete) from the Glimsoft Timelines app's cloud via the `tt` CLI. Use when the user asks what they're tracking, what they did today, their timelines, time per category, wants to start/stop/adjust a timer, or wants to log a finished/past activity.
- todoist-cli: Manage Todoist tasks, projects, labels, filters, sections, comments, reminders, and workspaces via the `td` CLI. Use when the user wants to view, create, update, complete, or organize Todoist items, or mentions tasks, inbox, today, upcoming, projects, labels, or filters.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll rebuild Things-Sync modeled on your Timelines-Sync project, using the things3-cli approach. Let me first look at all three: the current repo state, Timelines-Sync, and the things3-cli source.

input.command: git -C /Users/isak/Projects/Things-Sync log --oneline -10 && echo --- && git -C /Users/isak/Projects/Things-Sync show --stat HEAD | head -30 && echo --- && ls -la /Users/isak/Projects/Things-Sync
input.description: Inspect Things-Sync repo history and current state

input.command: find /Users/isak/Projects/Timelines-Sync -type f -not -path '*/.git/*' -not -path '*/.venv/*' -not -path '*/__pycache__/*' | head -50
input.description: List files in Timelines-Sync project

input.description: Show old Things-Sync README, pyproject, and file sizes from git HEAD

content.content:
Exit code 1
# things-sync

Python wrapper for Things 3. Two layers, by what each is best at:

- **`Things`** — façade. Writes go through AppleScript (`osascript`),
  reads through the SQLite layer below. Synchronous against Things'
  local store: a write returns once Things has committed it, so a
  follow-up read sees it immediately.
- **`ThingsDB`** — read-only SQLite at disk speed, reading Things' own
  database. Same dataclasses as the façade returns from writes.

Things 3 must be installed and **running** on the local Mac. There is
no networked / cloud-direct write path — every write is an AS call
against the local app. Reads work whether the app is foreground or
background, but the app process must be alive for writes.

## Install

```bash
uv sync --extra dev
```

(Or, if vendoring into another project, `uv add` from a local path.)

Zero non-stdlib runtime dependencies.

## Quickstart

```python
from things_sync import Things

t = Things()

project = t.create_project("Plan trip", deadline="2026-05-01")
todo = t.create_todo(
    "Book flights",
    notes="aisle seat please",
    deadline="2026-04-29",
    tags=["travel"],
    project=project.id,
)

t.update_todo(todo.id, notes="window seat actually")
t.complete(todo.id)

t.delete(todo.id)        # → Trash (recoverable)
t.empty_trash()          # purge
```

## Entity & operation matrix

| Entity   | Create | Read | Edit fields | Status | Trash / purge |
|----------|--------|------|-------------|--------|---------------|
| Todo     | AS     | DB   | name, notes, due_date, when, tags, project, area, contact | open / completed / canceled | `delete` + `empty_trash` |
| Project  | AS     | DB   | name, notes, due_date, tags, area | open / completed / canceled | `delete` + `empty_trash` |
| Area     | AS     | DB   | name, tags, collapsed | n/a | `delete` |
| Tag      | AS     | DB   | name, shortcut, parent | n/a | `delete` |
| Contact  | AS     | DB   | not exposed | n/a | not exposed |
| Heading  | Shortcut | DB | not exposed | n/a | Shortcut |

### Editing semantics

`update_todo` / `update_project` / `update_area` / `update_tag` accept
keyword arguments only. Pass a value to set it; **omit** the keyword to
leave the field alone.

```python
t.update_todo(todo.id, name="Book trains instead", notes="rebook",
              due_date="2026-05-15", tags=["travel", "urgent"])
t.update_project(project.id, name="Plan adventure",
                 due_date="2026-06-01", area=area.id)
t.update_area(area.id, name="Personal", collapsed=True)
t.update_tag(tag.id, name="errands", shortcut="e")
```

For nullable parents on todos (`project=None`, `area=None`,
`contact=None`) — passing `None` clears the parent. Clearing
`project` or `area` routes the todo back to Inbox (Things' AS
move-to-list, which clears project + area + heading at once).

### Status moves

```python
t.complete(todo.id)
t.cancel(todo.id)
t.reopen(todo.id)
```

For projects, status goes through `update_project(id, status=…)` with
`Status.OPEN` / `Status.COMPLETED` / `Status.CANCELED`.

### Schedule / move

`when` is the start/scheduled date that decides which built-in list
(Today / Upcoming / Anytime / Someday) the todo shows up in.

```python
from datetime import date

t.schedule(todo.id, date.today())            # → Today
t.schedule(todo.id, "2026-05-10")            # → Upcoming, then Today on the day
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Anytime")           # clear schedule
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Someday")
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Inbox")             # also clears project/area
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Trash")             # → soft-trash
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Logbook")           # → complete

t.move_to_project(todo.id, project.id)
t.move_to_area(todo.id, area.id)
```

`Upcoming` is derived from a future schedule date — use `schedule`,
not `move_to_list("Upcoming")` (which raises).

### Deletion

```python
t.delete(todo.id)            # soft, → Trash, recoverable
t.empty_trash()              # purges all currently-trashed items
t.delete_immediately(todo.id)  # convenience: trash + empty
```

`delete` works on todos, projects, areas, and tags. (Tags and areas
are removed outright; todos and projects move to Trash.)

## API surface

All methods live on `Things`. Identifiers are Things' own UUIDs
(returned on every create / read).

**Reads.** `version`, `count_todos`, `count_projects`, `count_areas`,
`count_tags`, `lists`, `todos`, `todo`, `todos_in_list`,
`todos_in_project`, `todos_in_area`, `todos_with_tag`, `selected_todos`,
`projects`, `project`, `headings`, `areas`, `area`, `tags`, `tag`,
`contacts`, `exists`.

**Create.** `create_todo`, `create_project`, `create_area`,
`create_tag`, `create_contact`, `parse_quicksilver`.

**Update.** `update_todo`, `update_project`, `update_area`, `update_tag`.

**Status.** `complete`, `cancel`, `reopen`.

**Move / schedule.** `move_to_list`, `move_to_area`, `move_to_project`,
`schedule`.

**Delete.** `delete` (→ Trash for todos/projects, outright for areas/
tags), `empty_trash`, `delete_immediately`.

**UI.** `show`, `edit`, `show_quick_entry`, `launch`, `quit`,
`is_running`.

**Maintenance.** `log_completed_now`.

## Fast reads (`ThingsDB`)

AppleScript enumeration is dominated by per-property IPC roundtrips —
reading every todo with all fields can take seconds per hundred items.
`ThingsDB` skips AppleScript entirely and reads Things' on-disk SQLite
file directly. Same dataclasses, same fields, 100–1000× faster:

```python
from things_sync import ThingsDB, Status

db = ThingsDB()                      # autodetects ~/Library/Group Containers/…
open_todos = [t for t in db.todos() if t.status == Status.OPEN]
projects   = db.projects()
headings   = db.headings()
areas      = db.areas()
tags       = db.tags()
```

The DB is opened in `mode=ro` and is WAL-mode, so reading while the
app is running is safe. Pass `ThingsDB(path=…)` if your install lives
in a non-standard container.

## Models

Frozen dataclasses in `things_sync.models`: `Todo`, `Project`, `Heading`,
`Area`, `Tag`, `Contact`, `ListInfo`, plus the `Status` enum
(`open`/`completed`/`canceled`) and `StartBucket`
(`inbox`/`anytime`/`someday`).

## Known limits

The AS dictionary doesn't cover everything Things' UI exposes; we
keep the wrapper to what AS can actually do reliably:

- **Headings: create + delete via Shortcuts; rename UI-only.**
  AppleScript has no `heading` class at all — neither `make new
  heading` nor `delete (heading id ...)` parse. Both create and
  delete route through Shortcuts.app:
  `Things.create_heading(project_id, name)` shells out to
  `shortcuts run "Things-Sync Add Heading"`, and
  `Things.delete_heading(heading_id)` shells out to
  `shortcuts run "Things-Sync Delete Heading"`. Both Shortcuts
  must be set up once by the user — see the docstrings on the
  two methods for the action recipes. Renaming a heading and
  reassigning todos under one are still UI-only.
- **No programmatic date clearing.** AS rejects
  `set due date of t to missing value` for date-typed properties.
  `update_todo(id, due_date=None)` raises. Clear due dates from
  the Things UI.
- **No `lists` collection enumeration.** The AS `lists` accessor is
  broken on Things 3.22.11; `Things.lists()` returns built-in names
  hardcoded, and `todos_in_list(name)` reads via SQLite.

## Tests

```bash
uv run pytest
```

Live tests run against the Things 3 instance on this machine. Every
created entity is name-prefixed `_ts_test_` and purged in teardown.

**Do not run against a Things account holding real data.** Cleanup
is best-effort and AS bugs in the test suite can leave residue under
the `_ts_test_` prefix.

## How it works

Source layout:

```
src/things_sync/
  __init__.py     — public exports
  models.py       — dataclasses
  things.py       — Things class (façade, AS writers)
  _db.py          — ThingsDB (read-only SQLite)
  _osascript.py   — osascript runner + delimited-output parser
  _scripts.py     — AppleScript prelude + per-class serializers
```

Writes pipe an AppleScript through `osascript -`. The prelude defines
field/record separators (ASCII US `\x1f` and RS `\x1e`) and per-class
serializers; write bodies build a `tell {Things3}` block, mutate the
target entity, and return its serialized record. Reads through
`ThingsDB` open Things' SQLite at
`~/Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/`
in read-only mode.

# things-sync

Python wrapper for Things 3. Two layers, by what each is best at:

- **`Things`** — façade. Writes go through AppleScript (`osascript`),
  reads through the SQLite layer below. Synchronous against Things'
  local store: a write returns once Things has committed it, so a
  follow-up read sees it immediately.
- **`ThingsDB`** — read-only SQLite at disk speed, reading Things' own
  database. Same dataclasses as the façade returns from writes.

Things 3 must be installed and **running** on the local Mac. There is
no networked / cloud-direct write path — every write is an AS call
against the local app. Reads work whether the app is foreground or
background, but the app process must be alive for writes.

## Install

```bash
uv sync --extra dev
```

(Or, if vendoring into another project, `uv add` from a local path.)

Zero non-stdlib runtime dependencies.

## Quickstart

```python
from things_sync import Things

t = Things()

project = t.create_project("Plan trip", deadline="2026-05-01")
todo = t.create_todo(
    "Book flights",
    notes="aisle seat please",
    deadline="2026-04-29",
    tags=["travel"],
    project=project.id,
)

t.update_todo(todo.id, notes="window seat actually")
t.complete(todo.id)

t.delete(todo.id)        # → Trash (recoverable)
t.empty_trash()          # purge
```

content.content:
1	# things3-cli
2	
3	[![CI](https://github.com/ossianhempel/things3-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ossianhempel/things3-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
4	
5	CLI for Things 3 by Cultured Code, implemented in Go.
6	
7	This project ships a single Go binary with unit and integration tests.
8	
9	## Status
10	
11	Work in progress. The goal is full end-to-end coverage for the Things URL
12	scheme interactions on macOS.
13	
14	## Installation (from source)
15	
16	```
17	make install
18	```
19	
20	## Installation (Homebrew)
21	
22	```
23	brew install ossianhempel/tap/things3-cli
24	```
25	
26	## Features
27	
28	- `add`              Add a new todo
29	- `update`           Update an existing todo (requires auth token)
30	- `delete`           Delete an existing todo
31	- `add-area`         Add a new area
32	- `add-project`      Add a new project
33	- `update-area`      Update an existing area
34	- `delete-area`      Delete an existing area
35	- `update-project`   Update an existing project (requires auth token)
36	- `list-project-tasks` List tasks for a project ID from the database
37	- `rename-project`   Rename an existing project (requires auth token)
38	- `delete-project`   Delete an existing project
39	- `show`             Show an area, project, tag, or todo from the database (`--recursive` includes checklist items for todos)
40	- `search`           Search todos and projects in the database
41	- `inbox`            List inbox tasks
42	- `today`            List today tasks
43	- `upcoming`         List upcoming tasks
44	- `repeating`        List repeating tasks
45	- `templates`        List repeating template tasks
46	- `anytime`          List anytime tasks
47	- `someday`          List someday tasks
48	- `logbook`          List logbook tasks
49	- `logtoday`         List tasks completed today
50	- `createdtoday`     List tasks created today
51	- `completed`        List completed tasks
52	- `canceled`         List canceled tasks
53	- `trash`            List trashed tasks
54	- `deadlines`        List tasks with deadlines
55	- `all`              List key sections from the database
56	- `help`             Command help and man page
57	- `--version`        Print CLI + Things version info
58	
59	## Auth token setup (for URL-scheme updates)
60	
61	Ordinary update operations use the Things URL scheme and require an auth token.
62	Repeat-only updates write directly to the database and do not require the URL
63	token. A command that combines ordinary fields with repeat fields requires both
64	the token and writable database access.
65	
66	1. Open Things 3.
67	2. Settings -> General -> Things URLs.
68	3. Copy the token (or enable "Allow 'things' CLI to access Things").
69	4. Export it:
70	
71	```
72	export THINGS_AUTH_TOKEN=your_token_here
73	```
74	
75	Tip: add the export to your shell profile (e.g. `~/.zshrc`) to persist it.
76	You can run `things auth` to check token status and print these steps.
77	
78	## Database access
79	
80	In addition to the URL-scheme commands above, this CLI can read your local
81	Things database to list content:
82	
83	- `things projects`  List projects
84	- `things areas`     List areas
85	- `things tags`      List tags
86	- `things tasks`     List todos (with filters)
87	- `things today`     List Today tasks
88	- `things templates` List repeating template tasks
89	- `things list-project-tasks --id <UUID>` List todos for a project
90	
91	`things today` follows Things' Today/This Evening ordering using the raw
92	`today_index_reference_date` and `today_index` database fields. Select the
93	ordering metadata with `--select start_bucket,today_index_reference_date,today_index`.
94	
95	By default it looks for the Things database in your user Library under the
96	Things app group container (the `ThingsData-*` folder). You can override the
97	path with `THINGSDB` or `--db`.
98	
99	Read commands need database access; repeat commands need writable database
100	access. Because the database lives inside the Things app sandbox, both normally
101	require Full Disk Access for your terminal or agent host.
102	
103	## Repeating todos
104	
105	Use `--repeat` flags with `add` or `update`
106	to create or change repeating templates. These changes write directly to the
107	Things database, so Full Disk Access is required. Repeating updates require a
108	single explicit title (for add) or `--id` (for update).
109	
110	Use `things templates` to list the hidden template rows that control future
111	instances of recurring todos. Template UUIDs can be passed to `things update`
112	when you need to move or edit the source template rather than the visible
113	generated instance.
114	
115	Supported patterns are every N day/week/month/year. Use after-completion mode
116	(the default) when the next copy should be based on completing the current copy;
117	use schedule mode for a fixed calendar cadence. `--repeat-start` anchors the
118	recurrence pattern and is separate from the todo's ordinary `--when` date.
119	`--repeat-deadline=N` adds a deadline so each copy appears in Today N days
120	earlier. Multi-day weekly patterns are not supported yet. Use `--repeat-until`
121	to stop after a date. Repeating projects are not supported.
122	
123	`--dry-run` previews the normalized repeat operation without opening Things or
124	writing the database. After a real write, the CLI re-reads the template and only
125	reports verified repeat state. If a multi-stage add partially succeeds, its
126	output identifies the completed and failed stages and includes a trusted UUID
127	when available; re-read that UUID before retrying instead of guessing by title.
128	
129	Examples:
130	
131	```
132	things add "Daily standup" --repeat=day --repeat-mode=schedule
133	things update --id <uuid> --repeat=week --repeat-every=2
134	things update --id <uuid> --repeat-clear
135	things update --id <uuid> --complete-checklist-item "Book hotel"
136	things update --id <uuid> --incomplete-checklist-item "Book hotel"
137	```
138	
139	## Agent Skills
140	
141	This repo includes a Things agent skill at `skills/things/SKILL.md`.
142	
143	That canonical file is mirrored to `../agent-scripts/skills/things/SKILL.md` so
144	local agent setups can consume the same guidance. Run `make check-things-skill`
145	to check a sibling checkout and `make sync-things-skill` to update the exact
146	active mirror safely.
147	
148	The `skill-sync.yml` workflow runs on every `main` push and compares both
149	repositories' published `main` artifacts. It uses a read-only deploy key stored
150	as the `AGENT_SCRIPTS_DEPLOY_KEY` Actions secret to fetch the private
151	`ossianhempel/agent-scripts` mirror.
152	
153	Maintainer release guidance lives in `.codex/skills/release-flow/SKILL.md` and
154	`docs/RELEASING.md`. Use it when preparing a public release or updating the
155	Homebrew formula.
156	
157	## Notes
158	
159	- macOS only (uses the Things URL scheme and `open` under the hood).
160	- Authentication for update operations follows the Things URL scheme
161	  authorization model.
162	- Write commands open Things in the background by default; use `--foreground`
163	  to bring it to the front, or `--dry-run` to print the URL without opening.
164	- `show --recursive` includes checklist items in both table and JSON output.
165	- Delete commands (todo/project/area) use AppleScript and require Things
166	  automation permission for your terminal (you may see a macOS prompt).
167	- Delete commands prompt for confirmation when run interactively; pass
168	  `--confirm` in non-interactive scripts. Use `--dry-run` to preview.
169	- Aliases: `create-project` -> `add-project`, `create-area` -> `add-area`.
170	- Scheduling: use `--when=someday` to move to Someday; use `update --later`
171	  (or `--when=evening`) to move to This Evening.

# things3-cli

[![CI](https://github.com/ossianhempel/things3-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ossianhempel/things3-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

CLI for Things 3 by Cultured Code, implemented in Go.

This project ships a single Go binary with unit and integration tests.

## Status

Work in progress. The goal is full end-to-end coverage for the Things URL
scheme interactions on macOS.

## Installation (from source)

```
make install
```

## Installation (Homebrew)

```
brew install ossianhempel/tap/things3-cli
```

## Features

- `add`              Add a new todo
- `update`           Update an existing todo (requires auth token)
- `delete`           Delete an existing todo
- `add-area`         Add a new area
- `add-project`      Add a new project
- `update-area`      Update an existing area
- `delete-area`      Delete an existing area
- `update-project`   Update an existing project (requires auth token)
- `list-project-tasks` List tasks for a project ID from the database
- `rename-project`   Rename an existing project (requires auth token)
- `delete-project`   Delete an existing project
- `show`             Show an area, project, tag, or todo from the database (`--recursive` includes checklist items for todos)
- `search`           Search todos and projects in the database
- `inbox`            List inbox tasks
- `today`            List today tasks
- `upcoming`         List upcoming tasks
- `repeating`        List repeating tasks
- `templates`        List repeating template tasks
- `anytime`          List anytime tasks
- `someday`          List someday tasks
- `logbook`          List logbook tasks
- `logtoday`         List tasks completed today
- `createdtoday`     List tasks created today
- `completed`        List completed tasks
- `canceled`         List canceled tasks
- `trash`            List trashed tasks
- `deadlines`        List tasks with deadlines
- `all`              List key sections from the database
- `help`             Command help and man page
- `--version`        Print CLI + Things version info

## Auth token setup (for URL-scheme updates)

Ordinary update operations use the Things URL scheme and require an auth token.
Repeat-only updates write directly to the database and do not require the URL
token. A command that combines ordinary fields with repeat fields requires both
the token and writable database access.

1. Open Things 3.
2. Settings -> General -> Things URLs.
3. Copy the token (or enable "Allow 'things' CLI to access Things").
4. Export it:

```
export THINGS_AUTH_TOKEN=your_token_here
```

Tip: add the export to your shell profile (e.g. `~/.zshrc`) to persist it.
You can run `things auth` to check token status and print these steps.

## Database access

In addition to the URL-scheme commands above, this CLI can read your local
Things database to list content:

- `things projects`  List projects
- `things areas`     List areas
- `things tags`      List tags
- `things tasks`     List todos (with filters)
- `things today`     List Today tasks
- `things templates` List repeating template tasks
- `things list-project-tasks --id <UUID>` List todos for a project

`things today` follows Things' Today/This Evening ordering using the raw
`today_index_reference_date` and `today_index` database fields. Select the
ordering metadata with `--select start_bucket,today_index_reference_date,today_index`.

By default it looks for the Things database in your user Library under the
Things app group container (the `ThingsData-*` folder). You can override the
path with `THINGSDB` or `--db`.

Read commands need database access; repeat commands need writable database
access. Because the database lives inside the Things app sandbox, both normally
require Full Disk Access for your terminal or agent host.

## Repeating todos

Use `--repeat` flags with `add` or `update`
to create or change repeating templates. These changes write directly to the
Things database, so Full Disk Access is required. Repeating updates require a
single explicit title (for add) or `--id` (for update).

Use `things templates` to list the hidden template rows that control future
instances of recurring todos. Template UUIDs can be passed to `things update`
when you need to move or edit the source template rather than the visible
generated instance.

Supported patterns are every N day/week/month/year. Use after-completion mode
(the default) when the next copy should be based on completing the current copy;
use schedule mode for a fixed calendar cadence. `--repeat-start` anchors the
recurrence pattern and is separate from the todo's ordinary `--when` date.
`--repeat-deadline=N` adds a deadline so each copy appears in Today N days
earlier. Multi-day weekly patterns are not supported yet. Use `--repeat-until`
to stop after a date. Repeating projects are not supported.

`--dry-run` previews the normalized repeat operation without opening Things or
writing the database. After a real write, the CLI re-reads the template and only
reports verified repeat state. If a multi-stage add partially succeeds, its
output identifies the completed and failed stages and includes a trusted UUID
when available; re-read that UUID before retrying instead of guessing by title.

Examples:

```
things add "Daily standup" --repeat=day --repeat-mode=schedule
things update --id <uuid> --repeat=week --repeat-every=2
things update --id <uuid> --repeat-clear
things update --id <uuid> --complete-checklist-item "Book hotel"
things update --id <uuid> --incomplete-checklist-item "Book hotel"
```

## Agent Skills

This repo includes a Things agent skill at `skills/things/SKILL.md`.

That canonical file is mirrored to `../agent-scripts/skills/things/SKILL.md` so
local agent setups can consume the same guidance. Run `make check-things-skill`
to check a sibling checkout and `make sync-things-skill` to update the exact
active mirror safely.

The `skill-sync.yml` workflow runs on every `main` push and compares both
repositories' published `main` artifacts. It uses a read-only deploy key stored
as the `AGENT_SCRIPTS_DEPLOY_KEY` Actions secret to fetch the private
`ossianhempel/agent-scripts` mirror.

Maintainer release guidance lives in `.codex/skills/release-flow/SKILL.md` and
`docs/RELEASING.md`. Use it when preparing a public release or updating the
Homebrew formula.

## Notes

- macOS only (uses the Things URL scheme and `open` under the hood).
- Authentication for update operations follows the Things URL scheme
  authorization model.
- Write commands open Things in the background by default; use `--foreground`
  to bring it to the front, or `--dry-run` to print the URL without opening.
- `show --recursive` includes checklist items in both table and JSON output.
- Delete commands (todo/project/area) use AppleScript and require Things
  automation permission for your terminal (you may see a macOS prompt).
- Delete commands prompt for confirmation when run interactively; pass
  `--confirm` in non-interactive scripts. Use `--dry-run` to preview.
- Aliases: `create-project` -> `add-project`, `create-area` -> `add-area`.
- Scheduling: use `--when=someday` to move to Someday; use `update --later`
  (or `--when=evening`) to move to This Evening.

1	"""`tt` CLI — a self-contained interface to the Timelines.app store
2	and its cloud sync backend.
3	"""
4	
5	from __future__ import annotations
6	
7	import json
8	import re
9	import sys
10	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
11	from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
12	from types import SimpleNamespace
13	from typing import Callable
14	
15	from rich import box
16	from rich.console import Console
17	from rich.table import Table
18	from rich.text import Text
19	
20	from timetrack import Event, Timeline
21	from timetrack import api, changes
22	from timetrack.sync import (
23	    Countdown,
24	    CountdownOp,
25	    add_countdown_preset,
26	    add_event,
27	    delete_event,
28	    edit_event,
29	    fetch_cloud_state,
30	    list_countdowns,
31	    local_aware,
32	    remove_countdown_preset,
33	    start_countdown,
34	    start_timer,
35	    stop_countdown,
36	    stop_timer,
37	)
38	
39	console = Console()
40	err_console = Console(stderr=True)
41	
42	
43	class CliError(Exception):
44	    """A user-facing argument-parsing error."""
45	
46	
47	@dataclass
48	class Flag:
49	    name: str            # long option, e.g. "--today"
50	    takes_value: bool
51	    help: str
52	    metavar: str = ""    # placeholder shown in help for value flags
53	
54	    @property
55	    def attr(self) -> str:
56	        return self.name.lstrip("-").replace("-", "_")
57	
58	
59	@dataclass
60	class Arg:
61	    name: str            # positional name, e.g. "timeline"
62	    help: str
63	    required: bool = True
64	    variadic: bool = False   # captures all remaining positionals, space-joined
65	
66	
67	@dataclass
68	class Command:
69	    name: str
70	    help: str
71	    func: Callable[[SimpleNamespace], int]
72	    flags: list[Flag] = field(default_factory=list)
73	    args: list[Arg] = field(default_factory=list)
74	
75	
76	def color_hex(color: int | None) -> str | None:
77	    """Decode a Timelines RGB565-packed color int into a '#RRGGBB' string
78	    usable as a rich style. Returns None for a falsy/zero color."""
79	    if not color:
80	        return None
81	    r = (color >> 11) & 0x1F
82	    g = (color >> 5) & 0x3F
83	    b = color & 0x1F
84	    return f"#{round(r * 255 / 31):02X}{round(g * 255 / 63):02X}{round(b * 255 / 31):02X}"
85	
86	
87	def _effective_end(e: Event, now: datetime) -> datetime | None:
88	    """End used for duration/overlap: 'now' for a running timer, else the
89	    stored end (falling back to the start)."""
90	    if e.time_tracking_active:
91	        return now
92	    if e.end_date:
93	        return local_aware(e.end_date)
94	    return local_aware(e.start_date) if e.start_date else None
95	
96	
97	def overlapping_uids(events: list[Event], now: datetime) -> set[str]:
98	    """UIDs of events whose [start, effective-end) interval intersects another's.
99	    Sweep in start order, tracking the furthest end seen so far."""
100	    items = []
101	    for e in events:
102	        if not e.start_date:
103	            continue
104	        s = local_aware(e.start_date)
105	        end = _effective_end(e, now) or s
106	        items.append((s, max(end, s), e.unique_identifier))
107	    items.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
108	    overlap: set[str] = set()
109	    max_end: datetime | None = None
110	    max_uid: str | None = None
111	    for s, end, uid in items:
112	        if max_end is not None and s < max_end:
113	            overlap.add(uid)
114	            if max_uid:
115	                overlap.add(max_uid)
116	        if max_end is None or end > max_end:
117	            max_end, max_uid = end, uid
118	    return overlap
119	
120	
121	def events_table(events: list[Event]) -> Table:
122	    """Render events as a table: color-swatched by timeline, running rows green
123	    (with a live duration), and time-overlapping rows red."""
124	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
125	    overlap = overlapping_uids(events, now)
126	    table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE_HEAD, header_style="bold", expand=False)
127	    table.add_column("ID", style="dim", no_wrap=True)
128	    table.add_column("Start", no_wrap=True)
129	    table.add_column("Dur", justify="right", no_wrap=True)
130	    table.add_column("Timeline", no_wrap=True)
131	    table.add_column("Name")
132	    for e in events:
133	        start = e.start_date.astimezone().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") if e.start_date else "?"
134	        eff_end = _effective_end(e, now)
135	        if e.start_date and eff_end is not None:
136	            dur = fmt_duration((eff_end - local_aware(e.start_date)).total_seconds())
137	            if e.time_tracking_active:
138	                dur += "…"  # ongoing
139	        else:
140	            dur = ""
141	        hexc = color_hex(e.marker_color)
142	        tl = Text()
143	        if hexc:
144	            tl.append("● ", style=hexc)
145	        tl.append(e.timeline_name or "?")
146	        name = Text(e.name) if e.name else Text("(unnamed)", style="dim")
147	        if e.unique_identifier in overlap:
148	            row_style = "red"          # overlap warning wins over the running highlight
149	        elif e.time_tracking_active:
150	            row_style = "bold green"
151	        else:
152	            row_style = None
153	        table.add_row(e.unique_identifier[:8], start, dur, tl, name, style=row_style)
154	    return table
155	
156	
157	def timelines_table(tls: list[Timeline]) -> Table:
158	    """Render timelines as a table with a color swatch and decoded hex."""
159	    table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE_HEAD, header_style="bold", expand=False)
160	    table.add_column("ID", style="dim", no_wrap=True)
161	    table.add_column("", no_wrap=True)  # color swatch
162	    table.add_column("Name")
163	    table.add_column("Color", style="dim", no_wrap=True)
164	    table.add_column("Flags", no_wrap=True)
165	    for t in tls:
166	        hexc = color_hex(t.color)
167	        swatch = Text("●", style=hexc) if hexc else Text(" ")
168	        flags = []
169	        if t.archived:
170	            flags.append("[yellow]archived[/yellow]")
171	        if not t.visible:
172	            flags.append("[dim]hidden[/dim]")
173	        table.add_row(
174	            t.unique_identifier[:8],
175	            swatch,
176	            Text(t.name, style=hexc or ""),
177	            hexc or str(t.color),
178	            Text.from_markup(" ".join(flags)) if flags else "",
179	        )
180	    return table
181	
182	
183	def _clock_time(s: str) -> tuple[int, int] | None:
184	    """Parse a bare clock time — 'HH:MM' or colon-less 'HMM'/'HHMM' (e.g. 930,
185	    2356) — into (hour, minute). None if it isn't a valid clock time."""
186	    m = re.fullmatch(r"(\d{1,2}):(\d{1,2})", s) or re.fullmatch(r"(\d{1,2})(\d{2})", s)
187	    if m is None:
188	        return None
189	    hh, mm = int(m[1]), int(m[2])
190	    return (hh, mm) if hh < 24 and mm < 60 else None
191	
192	
193	def _is_when_token(tok: str) -> bool:
194	    """True if a bare word is unmistakably a time: 'now', a clock time
195	    (13:00 / 1300 / 930), or a '+/-N[smhd]' offset. Deliberately narrower than
196	    parse_when (no ISO dates) so ordinary name words are never mistaken."""
197	    if tok == "now":
198	        return True
199	    if re.fullmatch(r"[+-]\d+[smhd]?", tok):
200	        return True
201	    return _clock_time(tok) is not None
202	
203	
204	def split_trailing_when(name: str | None, at: str | None) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
205	    """Let the time ride as the last positional: `tt start Transition 13:00`.
206	    If --at wasn't given and the final word of the (variadic) name arg looks
207	    like a time, peel it off and return it as the `at` string."""
208	    if at is not None or not name:
209	        return name, at
210	    words = name.split()
211	    if _is_when_token(words[-1]):
212	        return " ".join(words[:-1]) or None, words[-1]
213	    return name, at
214	
215	
216	def parse_when(s: str) -> datetime:
217	    """Accept ISO8601, or HH:MM / HHMM (today), or 'now' / '+N[mh]' / '-N[mh]'."""
218	    s = s.strip()
219	    now = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).astimezone()
220	    if s in ("now", ""):
221	        return now
222	    if s.startswith(("+", "-")):
223	        sign = 1 if s[0] == "+" else -1
224	        rest = s[1:]
225	        unit = "m"
226	        if rest and rest[-1] in "smhd":
227	            unit = rest[-1]
228	            rest = rest[:-1]
229	        n = int(rest)
230	        delta = {
231	            "s": timedelta(seconds=n),
232	            "m": timedelta(minutes=n),
233	            "h": timedelta(hours=n),
234	            "d": timedelta(days=n),
235	        }[unit]
236	        return now + sign * delta
237	    hm = _clock_time(s)
238	    if hm is not None:
239	        return now.replace(hour=hm[0], minute=hm[1], second=0, microsecond=0)
240	    try:
241	        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(s)
242	    except ValueError:
243	        raise SystemExit(f"error: could not parse time {s!r}")
244	    if dt.tzinfo is None:
245	        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=now.tzinfo)
246	    return dt
247	
248	
249	def parse_duration(s: str) -> timedelta:
250	    """Parse a duration like '1h30m', '90m', '2h', '45' (bare number = minutes)."""
251	    s = s.strip().lower()
252	    if not s:
253	        raise SystemExit("error: empty duration")
254	    if s.isdigit():
255	        return timedelta(minutes=int(s))
256	    units = {"s": 1, "m": 60, "h": 3600, "d": 86400}
257	    total = 0
258	    num = ""
259	    matched = False
260	    for ch in s:
261	        if ch.isdigit():
262	            num += ch
263	        elif ch in units and num:
264	            total += int(num) * units[ch]
265	            num = ""
266	            matched = True
267	        else:
268	            raise SystemExit(f"error: could not parse duration {s!r}")
269	    if num or not matched:
270	        raise SystemExit(f"error: could not parse duration {s!r}")
271	    return timedelta(seconds=total)
272	
273	
274	def fmt_duration(seconds: float) -> str:
275	    s = int(seconds)
276	    if s < 60:
277	        return f"{s}s"
278	    m, s = divmod(s, 60)
279	    if m < 60:
280	        return f"{m}m{s:02d}s" if s else f"{m}m"
281	    h, m = divmod(m, 60)
282	    return f"{h}h{m:02d}m"
283	
284	
285	def cloud_state() -> tuple[dict[str, Event], dict[str, Timeline], int]:
286	    """Pull current cloud events + timelines (the single source of truth), or
287	    exit with a clear error. Needs only the auth env vars — no local app."""
288	    try:
289	        events, timelines, _cobjs, _client, counter = fetch_cloud_state()
290	    except Exception as e:
291	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] cloud fetch failed: {e}")
292	        raise SystemExit(1)
293	    return events, timelines, counter
294	
295	
296	# ---------- commands ----------
297	
298	
299	def cmd_status(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:
300	    events, timelines, counter = cloud_state()
301	    tls = list(timelines.values())
302	    active = sum(1 for t in tls if not t.archived)
303	    console.print(f"[bold]source:[/bold]    [dim]cloud.timelines.app (counter {counter})[/dim]")
304	    console.print(f"[bold]timelines:[/bold] {active} active [dim]({len(tls)} total)[/dim]")
305	    console.print(f"[bold]events:[/bold]    {len(events)}")
306	    running = next((e for e in events.values() if e.time_tracking_active), None)
307	    if running is not None:
308	        started = running.start_date.astimezone().strftime("%H:%M") if running.start_date else "?"
309	        elapsed = (
310	            fmt_duration((datetime.now(timezone.utc) - local_aware(running.start_date)).total_seconds())
311	            if running.start_date
312	            else "?"
313	        )
314	        line = Text("running:   ", style="bold")
315	        line.append("⏵ ", style="bold green")
316	        line.append(running.timeline_name or "?", style=color_hex(running.marker_color) or "")
317	        line.append(f"  {running.name or '(unnamed)'}")
318	        line.append(f"  {elapsed}", style="bold green")
319	        line.append(f"  since {started}", style="dim")
320	        console.print(line)
321	    else:
322	        console.print("[bold]running:[/bold]   [dim]none[/dim]")

326	def cmd_events(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:
327	    events_by_uid, _timelines, _counter = cloud_state()
328	    events = list(events_by_uid.values())
329	    if args.today:
330	        today = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
331	        events = [e for e in events if e.day_identifier == today]
332	    if args.since:
333	        cutoff = parse_when(args.since).astimezone(timezone.utc)
334	        events = [e for e in events if e.start_date and e.start_date >= cutoff]
335	    if args.past:
336	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
337	        events = [e for e in events if e.start_date and local_aware(e.start_date) <= now]
338	    if args.timeline:
339	        events = [
340	            e
341	            for e in events
342	            if (e.timeline_identifier and e.timeline_identifier.startswith(args.timeline.upper()))
343	            or e.timeline_name == args.timeline
344	        ]
345	    events.sort(key=lambda e: e.start_date or datetime.min.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc))
346	    if args.json:
347	        print(json.dumps([e.to_json_dict() for e in events], indent=2, default=str))
348	        return 0
349	    if not events:
350	        console.print("[dim]no events[/dim]")
351	        return 0
352	    console.print(events_table(events))
353	    return 0

370	def _fmt_change_ts(ts: str | None) -> str:
371	    """Render a change's ISO timestamp as local 'MM-DD HH:MM', best-effort."""
372	    if not ts:
373	        return "?"
374	    s = ts[:-1] + "+00:00" if ts.endswith("Z") else ts
375	    try:
376	        return datetime.fromisoformat(s).astimezone().strftime("%m-%d %H:%M")
377	    except ValueError:
378	        return ts
379	
380	
381	def changes_table(records: list[dict]) -> Table:
382	    """Render change records: when, what action, which event, which fields."""
383	    table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE_HEAD, header_style="bold", expand=False)
384	    table.add_column("When", no_wrap=True)
385	    table.add_column("Action", no_wrap=True)
386	    table.add_column("ID", style="dim", no_wrap=True)
387	    table.add_column("Fields")
388	    styles = {"create": "green", "edit": "yellow", "delete": "red"}
389	    for rec in records:
390	        action = rec.get("action", "?")
391	        fields = rec.get("changedFields") or {}
392	        summary = ", ".join(fields) if fields else ("—" if action == "delete" else "")
393	        table.add_row(
394	            _fmt_change_ts(rec.get("timestamp")),
395	            Text(action, style=styles.get(action, "")),
396	            (rec.get("uniqueIdentifier") or "?")[:8],
397	            summary,
398	        )
399	    return table
400

402	def cmd_changes(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:
403	    """Emit atomic event changes since a counter. Stateless: the caller stores
404	    the returned counter and decides what to do with the changes.
405	
406	    With no --since, reports just the cloud's current counter (a seed point).
407	    With --since N, returns every event change newer than N.
408	    """
409	    try:
410	        if args.since is None:
411	            counter, recs = changes.current_counter(), []
412	        else:
413	            counter, recs = changes.changes_since(int(args.since))
414	    except ValueError:
415	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] --since must be an integer")
416	        return 2
417	    except Exception as e:
418	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] cloud fetch failed: {e}")
419	        return 1
420	
421	    if args.json:
422	        print(json.dumps({"counter": counter, "changes": recs}, ensure_ascii=False))
423	        return 0
424	    if args.since is None:
425	        console.print(f"counter: [bold]{counter}[/bold]")
426	        return 0
427	    if not recs:
428	        console.print(f"[dim]no changes since {args.since} (counter {counter})[/dim]")
429	        return 0
430	    console.print(changes_table(recs))
431	    console.print(f"[dim]{len(recs)} change(s); counter now {counter}[/dim]")
432	    return 0

435	def cmd_auth(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:
436	    """Save a sync session to ~/.config/timelines/auth.json.
437	
438	    Since there's no real signin flow, this bootstraps the session from the
439	    TIMELINES_AUTH_TOKEN/TIMELINES_DEVICE_UUID env vars (loaded by direnv from
440	    .envrc), falling back to scraping the Timelines app's Cache.db."""
441	    try:
442	        if args.from_cache:
443	            auth = api.load_auth_from_cache()
444	            source = "app cache (Cache.db)"
445	        else:
446	            auth = api.load_auth_from_env()
447	            if auth is not None:
448	                source = f"env ({api.ENV_TOKEN}/{api.ENV_DEVICE_UUID})"
449	            else:
450	                auth = api.load_auth_from_cache()
451	                source = "app cache (Cache.db)"
452	    except Exception as e:
453	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] {e}")
454	        return 1
455	    path = api.save_auth(auth)
456	    console.print(f"[bold green]✓ saved session[/bold green] [dim]from {source}[/dim]")
457	    console.print(f"  [dim]→ {path}[/dim]")
458	    console.print(
459	        f"  [dim]device[/dim] {auth.device_uuid[:8]}…  [dim]token[/dim] …{auth.token[-6:]}"
460	    )
461	    return 0

464	def cmd_doctor(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:
465	    """Verify what the CLI needs: a usable auth session + cloud reachability."""
466	    ok = True
467	
468	    def check(label: str, passed: bool, detail: str = "") -> None:
469	        nonlocal ok
470	        mark = "[green]✓[/green]" if passed else "[red]✗[/red]"
471	        console.print(f"  {mark} {label}")
472	        if detail:
473	            console.print(f"      [dim]{detail}[/dim]")
474	        if not passed:
475	            ok = False
476	
477	    console.print("[bold]timelines doctor[/bold]")
478	    console.print()
479	    console.print("[bold underline]Auth[/bold underline]")
480	    env_auth = api.load_auth_from_env()
481	    file_auth = api.load_auth_from_file()
482	    auth = env_auth or file_auth
483	    if env_auth is not None:
484	        src = f"env ({api.ENV_TOKEN} + {api.ENV_DEVICE_UUID})"
485	    elif file_auth is not None:
486	        src = f"session file ({api.SESSION_PATH})"
487	    else:
488	        src = ""
489	    check(
490	        "auth available",
491	        auth is not None,
492	        f"from {src}, device={auth.device_uuid[:8]}…"
493	        if auth is not None
494	        else f"set the env vars via direnv, or run `{PROG} auth`",
495	    )
496	
497	    console.print()
498	    console.print("[bold underline]Cloud[/bold underline]")
499	    if auth is None:
500	        console.print(f"  [yellow]⊘[/yellow] [dim]skipped — set up auth first (`{PROG} auth`)[/dim]")
501	        ok = False
502	    else:
503	        try:
504	            counter = api.SyncClient().discover_counter()
505	            check("cloud.timelines.app reachable", True, f"globalCounter={counter}")
506	        except Exception as e:
507	            check("cloud.timelines.app reachable", False, str(e))
508	
509	    console.print()
510	    if ok:
511	        console.print("status: [bold green]OK[/bold green]")
512	    else:
513	        console.print(
514	            "status: [bold red]FAIL[/bold red] [dim]— fix the items above[/dim]"
515	        )
516	    return 0 if ok else 1

522	def _op_line(head_markup: str, e: Event, tail: str = "") -> Text:
523	    """A status line: controlled markup head + a color swatch + plain (escaped-
524	    by-construction) event fields appended as Text."""
525	    line = Text.from_markup(head_markup + " ")
526	    hexc = color_hex(e.marker_color)
527	    if hexc:
528	        line.append("● ", style=hexc)
529	    line.append(e.timeline_name or "?")
530	    if e.name:
531	        line.append(f"  {e.name}")
532	    if tail:
533	        line.append(f"  {tail}", style="dim")
534	    return line

537	def _span(e: Event) -> str:
538	    if not e.start_date:
539	        return ""
540	    s = e.start_date.astimezone().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
541	    if e.end_date:
542	        return s + "–" + e.end_date.astimezone().strftime("%H:%M")
543	    return s

546	def cmd_add(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:
547	    if not args.start:
548	        err_console.print("[bold red]error:[/bold red] add requires --start")
549	        return 2
550	    duration = getattr(args, "for")
551	    if args.end and duration:
552	        err_console.print("[bold red]error:[/bold red] pass either --end or --for, not both")
553	        return 2
554	    start = parse_when(args.start)
555	    if args.end:
556	        end = parse_when(args.end)
557	    elif duration:
558	        end = start + parse_duration(duration)
559	    else:
560	        err_console.print("[bold red]error:[/bold red] add requires --end or --for")
561	        return 2
562	    r = add_event(
563	        args.timeline,
564	        name=args.name or "",
565	        notes=args.notes or "",
566	        start=start,
567	        end=end,
568	        dry_run=args.dry_run,
569	    )
570	    if r.error:
571	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] {r.error}")
572	        return 1
573	    e = r.event
574	    dur = fmt_duration((local_aware(e.end_date) - local_aware(e.start_date)).total_seconds())
575	    head = "[dim]would add[/dim]" if r.dry_run else "[bold green]＋ added[/bold green]"
576	    console.print(_op_line(head, e, f"{_span(e)} ({dur})"))
577	    return 0

651	# ---------- countdown timers ----------
652	
653	
654	def _fmt_secs(seconds: float) -> str:
655	    """Compact m:ss / h:mm:ss for a countdown's remaining/duration."""
656	    s = max(0, int(round(seconds)))
657	    h, rem = divmod(s, 3600)
658	    m, s = divmod(rem, 60)
659	    return f"{h}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}" if h else f"{m}:{s:02d}"
660	
661	
662	def _cd_swatch(cd: Countdown) -> Text:
663	    line = Text()
664	    hexc = color_hex(cd.timeline_color)
665	    if hexc:
666	        line.append("● ", style=hexc)
667	    line.append(cd.timeline_name or "?")
668	    if cd.name:
669	        line.append(f"  {cd.name}")
670	    return line
671	
672	
673	def _cd_line(head_markup: str, cd: Countdown, tail: str = "") -> Text:
674	    line = Text.from_markup(head_markup + " ")
675	    line.append_text(_cd_swatch(cd))
676	    if tail:
677	        line.append(f"  {tail}", style="dim")
678	    return line

681	def countdowns_table(cds: list[Countdown]) -> Table:
682	    table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE_HEAD, header_style="bold", expand=False)
683	    table.add_column("Timer")
684	    table.add_column("Duration", no_wrap=True)
685	    table.add_column("Status", no_wrap=True)
686	    for cd in cds:
687	        if cd.running and cd.remaining_seconds is not None:
688	            status = Text(f"⏳ {_fmt_secs(cd.remaining_seconds)} left", style="green")
689	        else:
690	            status = Text("idle", style="dim")
691	        table.add_row(_cd_swatch(cd), _fmt_secs(cd.duration_seconds), status)
692	    return table

730	def cmd_countdown_start(args: SimpleNamespace) -> int:

987	def print_countdown_help() -> None:
988	    console.print(f"[bold]{PROG} countdown[/bold] — countdown timers (alias: cd)")
989	    console.print("[dim]Define and start countdown timers that ring at zero.[/dim]")

993	    )
994	    console.print()
995	    table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, show_header=False, expand=False, pad_edge=False)
996	    table.add_column("Subcommand", style="bold cyan", no_wrap=True)
997	    table.add_column("Description")
998	    for c in COUNTDOWN_SUBCOMMANDS:
999	        table.add_row(c.name.split(maxsplit=1)[1], c.help)
1000	    console.print(table)
1001	    console.print(f"Run [bold]{PROG} countdown <subcommand> -h[/bold] for options.")

1004	def run_countdown(argv: list[str]) -> int:
1005	    """Dispatch `tt countdown <sub>` to its subcommand (own help + parsing)."""
1006	    if not argv or argv[0] in ("-h", "--help", "help"):
1007	        print_countdown_help()
1008	        return 0
1009	    sub, rest = argv[0], argv[1:]
1010	    cmd = COUNTDOWN_BY_NAME.get(sub)
1011	    if cmd is None:
1012	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] unknown countdown subcommand {sub!r}")
1013	        console.print()
1014	        print_countdown_help()
1015	        return 2
1016	    try:
1017	        ns = parse_command(cmd, rest)
1018	    except CliError as e:
1019	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] {e}")
1020	        return 2
1021	    return cmd.func(ns)

1024	def _flag_label(f: Flag) -> str:
1025	    return f"{f.name} {f.metavar}".strip() if f.takes_value else f.name
1026	
1027	
1028	def _arg_token(a: Arg) -> str:
1029	    """Plain label, e.g. '<timeline>' or '[name…]'. Not markup-safe — wrap in
1030	    Text (table cells) or escape the leading bracket (markup strings)."""
1031	    tok = f"{a.name}…" if a.variadic else a.name
1032	    return f"<{tok}>" if a.required else f"[{tok}]"
1033	
1034	
1035	def _usage(cmd: Command) -> str:
1036	    # markup-string context: escape the leading '[' of optional tokens
1037	    parts = [_arg_token(a).replace("[", "\\[") for a in cmd.args]
1038	    parts += [f"\\[{_flag_label(f)}]" for f in cmd.flags]
1039	    return " ".join(parts)
1040	
1041	
1042	def print_help() -> None:
1043	    console.print(f"[bold]{PROG}[/bold] — Glimsoft Timelines, from the terminal")
1044	    console.print(f"[dim]{DESCRIPTION}[/dim]")
1045	    console.print()
1046	    console.print(f"[bold]Usage:[/bold] {PROG} [cyan]<command>[/cyan] [dim]\\[args/options][/dim]")
1047	    console.print()
1048	    table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, show_header=False, expand=False, pad_edge=False)
1049	    table.add_column("Command", style="bold cyan", no_wrap=True)
1050	    table.add_column("Description")
1051	    for c in COMMANDS:
1052	        table.add_row(c.name, c.help)
1053	    console.print(table)
1054	    console.print(f"Run [bold]{PROG} <command> -h[/bold] for command options.")
1055	
1056	
1057	def print_command_help(cmd: Command) -> None:
1058	    console.print(
1059	        f"[bold]Usage:[/bold] {PROG} [cyan]{cmd.name}[/cyan] [dim]{_usage(cmd)}[/dim]".rstrip()
1060	    )
1061	    console.print(f"[dim]{cmd.help}[/dim]")
1062	    rows = [(_arg_token(a), a.help) for a in cmd.args]
1063	    rows += [(_flag_label(f), f.help) for f in cmd.flags]
1064	    if rows:
1065	        console.print()
1066	        table = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, show_header=False, expand=False, pad_edge=False)
1067	        table.add_column("", style="bold", no_wrap=True)
1068	        table.add_column("Help")
1069	        for label, helptext in rows:
1070	            table.add_row(Text(label), helptext)  # Text: no markup parsing on labels
1071	        console.print(table)
1072	
1073	
1074	def parse_command(cmd: Command, argv: list[str]) -> SimpleNamespace:
1075	    """Parse a command's positionals + flags into a namespace. Raises CliError
1076	    on bad input; raises SystemExit(0) after printing help for -h/--help."""
1077	    ns = SimpleNamespace(**{f.attr: (None if f.takes_value else False) for f in cmd.flags})
1078	    for a in cmd.args:
1079	        setattr(ns, a.name, None)
1080	    by_name = {f.name: f for f in cmd.flags}
1081	    positionals: list[str] = []
1082	    i = 0
1083	    while i < len(argv):
1084	        tok = argv[i]
1085	        if tok in ("-h", "--help"):
1086	            print_command_help(cmd)
1087	            raise SystemExit(0)
1088	        if tok.startswith("--"):
1089	            name, eq, inline = tok.partition("=")
1090	            f = by_name.get(name)
1091	            if f is None:
1092	                raise CliError(f"unknown option {tok!r} for `{cmd.name}`")
1093	            if f.takes_value:
1094	                if eq:
1095	                    value = inline
1096	                else:
1097	                    i += 1
1098	                    if i >= len(argv):
1099	                        raise CliError(f"{name} requires a value")
1100	                    value = argv[i]
1101	                setattr(ns, f.attr, value)
1102	            else:
1103	                if eq:
1104	                    raise CliError(f"{name} takes no value")
1105	                setattr(ns, f.attr, True)
1106	        else:
1107	            positionals.append(tok)
1108	        i += 1
1109	    # map positionals onto declared args (last arg may be variadic)
1110	    for idx, a in enumerate(cmd.args):
1111	        if a.variadic:
1112	            rest = positionals[idx:]
1113	            setattr(ns, a.name, " ".join(rest) if rest else None)
1114	            break
1115	        if idx < len(positionals):
1116	            setattr(ns, a.name, positionals[idx])
1117	    else:
1118	        extra = positionals[len(cmd.args):]
1119	        if extra:
1120	            raise CliError(f"unexpected argument {extra[0]!r} for `{cmd.name}`")
1121	    for a in cmd.args:
1122	        if a.required and not getattr(ns, a.name):
1123	            raise CliError(f"missing argument <{a.name}> for `{cmd.name}`")
1124	    return ns
1125	
1126	
1127	def main() -> int:
1128	    argv = sys.argv[1:]
1129	    if not argv or argv[0] in ("-h", "--help", "help"):
1130	        print_help()
1131	        return 0
1132	    name, rest = argv[0], argv[1:]
1133	    if name in ("countdown", "cd"):
1134	        return run_countdown(rest)
1135	    if name == "completion":
1136	        # hidden internal plumbing (the shell calls it) — kept out of `tt --help`
1137	        from timetrack import completion
1138	
1139	        return completion.run(rest)
1140	    cmd = COMMANDS_BY_NAME.get(name)
1141	    if cmd is None:
1142	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] unknown command {name!r}")
1143	        console.print()
1144	        print_help()
1145	        return 2
1146	    try:
1147	        ns = parse_command(cmd, rest)
1148	    except CliError as e:
1149	        err_console.print(f"[bold red]error:[/bold red] {e}")
1150	        return 2
1151	    return cmd.func(ns)
1152	
1153	
1154	if __name__ == "__main__":
1155	    sys.exit(main())
