1	"""Read-only access to Things 3's on-disk SQLite database.
2	
3	Things keeps its state in a WAL-mode SQLite file at::
4	
5	    ~/Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/
6	        ThingsData-*/Things Database.thingsdatabase/main.sqlite
7	
8	Enumerating entities through AppleScript is dominated by per-property IPC
9	roundtrips — a few hundred milliseconds per item once you read more than a
10	handful of fields. The same data read from SQLite comes back in
11	milliseconds regardless of count. For any read-heavy workload (sync
12	planning, reporting, bulk diffs) this is 100–1000× faster than
13	:class:`Things`.
14	
15	Reads only. Writes must still go through :class:`Things` — poking Cultured
16	Code's private store directly would desynchronise Things' in-memory state
17	and its CloudKit sync. WAL mode makes concurrent reads safe while the app
18	is running.
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import sqlite3
23	from contextlib import closing
24	from datetime import datetime
25	from pathlib import Path
26	
27	from .models import Area, Contact, Heading, Project, StartBucket, Status, Tag, Todo
28	
29	
30	_DB_GLOB = (
31	    "Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/"
32	    "ThingsData-*/Things Database.thingsdatabase/main.sqlite"
33	)
34	
35	# TMTask.type
36	_TYPE_TODO = 0
37	_TYPE_PROJECT = 1
38	_TYPE_HEADING = 2
39	
40	# TMTask.status — the ints Things uses on disk, distinct from our Status enum.
41	_STATUS_BY_INT = {0: Status.OPEN, 2: Status.CANCELED, 3: Status.COMPLETED}
42	_STATUS_CANCELED = 2
43	_STATUS_COMPLETED = 3
44	
45	
46	def _default_db_path() -> Path:
47	    """Locate the Things 3 SQLite file in the current user's Library.
48	
49	    Things writes to a container-ID directory that changes between
50	    installs, so we glob. Multiple ThingsData-* directories can exist if
51	    the app has been reinstalled; we take the most recently modified one.
52	    """
53	    candidates = list(Path.home().glob(_DB_GLOB))
54	    if not candidates:
55	        raise FileNotFoundError(
56	            "Things 3 database not found under ~/Library/Group Containers. "
57	            "Pass `path=` explicitly if your install is non-standard."
58	        )
59	    candidates.sort(key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
60	    return candidates[-1]
61	
62	
63	def _decode_packed_date(v: int | None) -> datetime | None:
64	    """Things packs `deadline` / `startDate` as a 32-bit int:
65	    year in bits 16+, month in 12–15, day in 7–11. 0 / NULL = unset.
66	
67	    Returns a midnight datetime to match the AppleScript reader (which
68	    rounds through Things' own isoDate serializer with zeroed H/M/S).
69	    """
70	    if not v:
71	        return None
72	    y = v >> 16
73	    m = (v >> 12) & 0xF
74	    d = (v >> 7) & 0x1F
75	    if not (1 <= m <= 12 and 1 <= d <= 31 and y >= 1970):
76	        return None
77	    try:
78	        return datetime(y, m, d)
79	    except ValueError:
80	        return None
81	
82	
83	def _encode_packed_date(d) -> int:
84	    """Inverse of :func:`_decode_packed_date`. Accepts a date or datetime."""
85	    return (d.year << 16) | (d.month << 12) | (d.day << 7)
86	
87	
88	def _start_bucket(v: int | None) -> StartBucket:
89	    """Map TMTask.start (0/1/2) onto the StartBucket enum.
90	
91	    Unexpected values fall through to ANYTIME — Things' internal schema has
92	    historically grown new codes, and leaving a foreign value unpinned would
93	    crash every reader the next time Cultured Code ships one.
94	    """
95	    try:
96	        return StartBucket(v)
97	    except ValueError:
98	        return StartBucket.ANYTIME
99	
100	
101	def _from_unix(v: float | None) -> datetime | None:
102	    if v is None:
103	        return None
104	    try:
105	        return datetime.fromtimestamp(v)
106	    except (OverflowError, OSError, ValueError):
107	        return None
108	
109	
110	class ThingsDB:
111	    """Read-only view onto Things' SQLite database.
112	
113	    Mirrors the read surface of :class:`Things` (``todos``, ``projects``,
114	    ``areas``, ``tags``) but reads directly from disk. Returns the same
115	    dataclasses from :mod:`things_sync.models`, so call sites can swap
116	    between the two readers without downstream changes.
117	
118	    Parameters
119	    ----------
120	    path
121	        Path to ``main.sqlite``. Autodetected in the default Things
122	        container if omitted.
123	    """
124	
125	    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None) -> None:
126	        self.path = Path(path) if path else _default_db_path()
127	
128	    # ------------------------------------------------------------ internals
129	
130	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
131	        # `mode=ro` opens read-only and never touches the file. We avoid
132	        # `immutable=1` because Things mutates the DB while we're reading.
133	        uri = f"file:{self.path}?mode=ro"
134	        con = sqlite3.connect(uri, uri=True)
135	        con.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
136	        return con
137	
138	    # --------------------------------------------------------------- reads
139	
140	    def todos(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
141	        """Every non-trashed todo (type=0), any status.
142	
143	        Pass ``include_trashed=True`` to also return items in the Trash.
144	        Callers that only want active items should filter on
145	        ``status == Status.OPEN`` themselves — this method doesn't.
146	        """
147	        return self._tasks(_TYPE_TODO, include_trashed, _todo_from_row)
148	
149	    def projects(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Project]:
150	        """Every non-trashed project (type=1), any status."""
151	        return self._tasks(_TYPE_PROJECT, include_trashed, _project_from_row)
152	
153	    def headings(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Heading]:
154	        """Every non-trashed heading (type=2), any status.
155	
156	        A heading lives inside a project; its ``project_id`` is the parent
157	        project's UUID. Todos under a heading reference it via their
158	        ``heading`` column (exposed on :class:`Todo` as ``heading_id``).
159	        """
160	        sql = """
161	            SELECT uuid, title, status, project
162	            FROM TMTask
163	            WHERE type = ?
164	        """
165	        if not include_trashed:
166	            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
167	        sql += ' ORDER BY "index"'
168	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
169	            rows = con.execute(sql, (_TYPE_HEADING,)).fetchall()
170	        return [
171	            Heading(
172	                id=r["uuid"],
173	                name=r["title"] or "",
174	                project_id=r["project"] or None,
175	                status=_STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN),
176	            )
177	            for r in rows
178	        ]
179	
180	    def areas(self) -> list[Area]:
181	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
182	            rows = con.execute(
183	                'SELECT uuid, title FROM TMArea ORDER BY "index"'
184	            ).fetchall()
185	            tags_by_owner = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMAreaTag", "areas")
186	        return [
187	            Area(
188	                id=r["uuid"],
189	                name=r["title"] or "",
190	                tag_names=tags_by_owner.get(r["uuid"], ()),
191	                # TMArea doesn't persist the sidebar collapsed state in
192	                # the schema we know about; surface the default instead
193	                # of fabricating an unreliable value.
194	                collapsed=False,
195	            )
196	            for r in rows
197	        ]
198	
199	    def tags(self) -> list[Tag]:
200	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
201	            rows = con.execute(
202	                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag ORDER BY "index"'
203	            ).fetchall()
204	        return [
205	            Tag(
206	                id=r["uuid"],
207	                name=r["title"] or "",
208	                parent_id=r["parent"] or None,
209	                keyboard_shortcut=r["shortcut"] or "",
210	            )
211	            for r in rows
212	        ]
213	
214	    def contacts(self) -> list[Contact]:
215	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
216	            rows = con.execute(
217	                'SELECT uuid, displayName FROM TMContact ORDER BY "index"'
218	            ).fetchall()
219	        return [Contact(id=r["uuid"], name=r["displayName"] or "") for r in rows]
220	
221	    # --------------------------------------------------------- by-id lookups
222	
223	    def todo(self, id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = True) -> Todo | None:
224	        return self._task_by_id(id, _TYPE_TODO, include_trashed, _todo_from_row)
225	
226	    def project(self, id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = True) -> Project | None:
227	        return self._task_by_id(id, _TYPE_PROJECT, include_trashed, _project_from_row)
228	
229	    def heading(self, id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = True) -> Heading | None:
230	        sql = "SELECT uuid, title, status, project FROM TMTask WHERE uuid=? AND type=?"
231	        if not include_trashed:
232	            sql += " AND trashed=0"
233	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
234	            r = con.execute(sql, (id, _TYPE_HEADING)).fetchone()
235	        if r is None:
236	            return None
237	        return Heading(
238	            id=r["uuid"],
239	            name=r["title"] or "",
240	            project_id=r["project"] or None,
241	            status=_STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN),
242	        )
243	
244	    def area(self, id: str) -> Area | None:
245	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
246	            r = con.execute(
247	                "SELECT uuid, title FROM TMArea WHERE uuid=?", (id,)
248	            ).fetchone()
249	            tags_by_owner = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMAreaTag", "areas")
250	        if r is None:
251	            return None
252	        return Area(
253	            id=r["uuid"],
254	            name=r["title"] or "",
255	            tag_names=tags_by_owner.get(r["uuid"], ()),
256	            collapsed=False,
257	        )
258	
259	    def tag(self, name: str) -> Tag | None:
260	        """Look up by display name (Things tags are name-unique)."""
261	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
262	            r = con.execute(
263	                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag WHERE title=?', (name,)
264	            ).fetchone()
265	        if r is None:
266	            return None
267	        return Tag(
268	            id=r["uuid"],
269	            name=r["title"] or "",
270	            parent_id=r["parent"] or None,
271	            keyboard_shortcut=r["shortcut"] or "",
272	        )
273	
274	    def tag_by_id(self, id: str) -> Tag | None:
275	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
276	            r = con.execute(
277	                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag WHERE uuid=?', (id,)
278	            ).fetchone()
279	        if r is None:
280	            return None
281	        return Tag(
282	            id=r["uuid"],
283	            name=r["title"] or "",
284	            parent_id=r["parent"] or None,
285	            keyboard_shortcut=r["shortcut"] or "",
286	        )
287	
288	    # ------------------------------------------------------- filtered todos
289	
290	    def todos_in_project(self, project_id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
291	        return self._tasks_filtered(_TYPE_TODO, "project=?", (project_id,), include_trashed)
292	
293	    def todos_in_area(self, area_id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
294	        return self._tasks_filtered(_TYPE_TODO, "area=?", (area_id,), include_trashed)
295	
296	    def todos_under_heading(self, heading_id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
297	        return self._tasks_filtered(_TYPE_TODO, "heading=?", (heading_id,), include_trashed)
298	
299	    def todos_in_list(self, name: str) -> list[Todo]:
300	        """Built-in virtual list — Things derives these from TMTask columns
301	        rather than storing them. The AppleScript ``to dos of list "X"``
302	        path is broken in Things 3.22.11 (returns -1728), so we
303	        re-implement the derivation against SQLite.
304	
305	        Supported names (case-insensitive): Inbox, Today, Upcoming,
306	        Anytime, Someday, Logbook, Trash. Heuristic but matches the UI
307	        for the cases atlas cares about.
308	        """
309	        from datetime import date as _date
310	
311	        n = name.lower()
312	        today = _encode_packed_date(_date.today())
313	
314	        # base SELECT shared by all branches
315	        cols = (
316	            'uuid, title, notes, status, trashed, '
317	            'creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate, '
318	            'start, startDate, deadline, '
319	            'project, area, contact, heading'
320	        )
321	        if n == "trash":
322	            sql = f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask WHERE trashed=1 AND type=? ORDER BY "index"'
323	            params: tuple = (_TYPE_TODO,)
324	        elif n == "logbook":
325	            sql = (
326	                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
327	                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status IN (2, 3) ORDER BY stopDate DESC'
328	            )
329	            params = (_TYPE_TODO,)
330	        elif n == "inbox":
331	            sql = (
332	                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
333	                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 AND start=0 ORDER BY "index"'
334	            )
335	            params = (_TYPE_TODO,)
336	        elif n == "today":
337	            # Today = open + scheduled-and-arrived. Includes pinned via
338	            # todayIndexReferenceDate but leaving that for atlas to refine.
339	            sql = (
340	                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
341	                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 '
342	                'AND startDate IS NOT NULL AND startDate <= ? '
343	                'ORDER BY todayIndex'
344	            )
345	            params = (_TYPE_TODO, today)
346	        elif n == "upcoming":
347	            sql = (
348	                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
349	                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 '
350	                'AND startDate IS NOT NULL AND startDate > ? '
351	                'ORDER BY startDate, "index"'
352	            )
353	            params = (_TYPE_TODO, today)
354	        elif n == "anytime":
355	            # Active items in the Anytime bucket without a future schedule.
356	            sql = (
357	                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
358	                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 AND start=1 '
359	                'AND (startDate IS NULL OR startDate <= ?) ORDER BY "index"'
360	            )
361	            params = (_TYPE_TODO, today)
362	        elif n == "someday":
363	            sql = (
364	                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
365	                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 AND start=2 ORDER BY "index"'
366	            )
367	            params = (_TYPE_TODO,)
368	        else:
369	            raise ValueError(
370	                f"unknown built-in list {name!r}; expected one of "
371	                "Inbox, Today, Upcoming, Anytime, Someday, Logbook, Trash"
372	            )
373	
374	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
375	            rows = con.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
376	            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
377	        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]
378	
379	    def todos_with_tag(self, name: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
380	        sql = """
381	            SELECT TMTask.uuid AS uuid, TMTask.title AS title, TMTask.notes AS notes,
382	                   TMTask.status AS status, TMTask.trashed AS trashed,
383	                   TMTask.creationDate, TMTask.userModificationDate, TMTask.stopDate,
384	                   TMTask.start, TMTask.startDate, TMTask.deadline,
385	                   TMTask.project, TMTask.area, TMTask.contact, TMTask.heading
386	            FROM TMTask
387	            JOIN TMTaskTag ON TMTaskTag.tasks = TMTask.uuid
388	            JOIN TMTag ON TMTag.uuid = TMTaskTag.tags
389	            WHERE TMTask.type = ? AND TMTag.title = ?
390	        """
391	        if not include_trashed:
392	            sql += " AND TMTask.trashed = 0"
393	        sql += ' ORDER BY TMTask."index"'
394	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
395	            rows = con.execute(sql, (_TYPE_TODO, name)).fetchall()
396	            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
397	        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]
398	
399	    # --------------------------------------------------------- counts / exists
400	
401	    def count_todos(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> int:
402	        return self._count(_TYPE_TODO, include_trashed)
403	
404	    def count_projects(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> int:
405	        return self._count(_TYPE_PROJECT, include_trashed)
406	
407	    def count_areas(self) -> int:
408	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
409	            return con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMArea").fetchone()[0]
410	
411	    def count_tags(self) -> int:
412	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
413	            return con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMTag").fetchone()[0]
414	
415	    def exists(self, id: str) -> bool:
416	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
417	            for sql, params in (
418	                ("SELECT 1 FROM TMTask WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1", (id,)),
419	                ("SELECT 1 FROM TMArea WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1", (id,)),
420	                ("SELECT 1 FROM TMTag WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1", (id,)),
421	            ):
422	                if con.execute(sql, params).fetchone() is not None:
423	                    return True
424	        return False
425	
426	    # ---------------------------------------------------------- task helpers
427	
428	    def _task_by_id(self, id: str, type_: int, include_trashed: bool, parse):
429	        sql = """
430	            SELECT uuid, title, notes, status, trashed,
431	                   creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate,
432	                   start, startDate, deadline,
433	                   project, area, contact, heading
434	            FROM TMTask
435	            WHERE uuid=? AND type=?
436	        """
437	        if not include_trashed:
438	            sql += " AND trashed=0"
439	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
440	            row = con.execute(sql, (id, type_)).fetchone()
441	            if row is None:
442	                return None
443	            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
444	        return parse(row, tags_by_task)
445	
446	    def _tasks_filtered(self, type_: int, where: str, params: tuple, include_trashed: bool) -> list:
447	        sql = f"""
448	            SELECT uuid, title, notes, status, trashed,
449	                   creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate,
450	                   start, startDate, deadline,
451	                   project, area, contact, heading
452	            FROM TMTask
453	            WHERE type = ? AND {where}
454	        """
455	        if not include_trashed:
456	            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
457	        sql += ' ORDER BY "index"'
458	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
459	            rows = con.execute(sql, (type_, *params)).fetchall()
460	            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
461	        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]
462	
463	    def _count(self, type_: int, include_trashed: bool) -> int:
464	        sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMTask WHERE type = ?"
465	        if not include_trashed:
466	            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
467	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
468	            return con.execute(sql, (type_,)).fetchone()[0]
469	
470	    def _tasks(self, type_: int, include_trashed: bool, parse) -> list:
471	        sql = """
472	            SELECT uuid, title, notes, status, trashed,
473	                   creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate,
474	                   start, startDate, deadline,
475	                   project, area, contact, heading
476	            FROM TMTask
477	            WHERE type = ?
478	        """
479	        if not include_trashed:
480	            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
481	        sql += ' ORDER BY "index"'
482	        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
483	            rows = con.execute(sql, (type_,)).fetchall()
484	            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
485	        return [parse(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]
486	
487	
488	def _tags_by_owner(
489	    con: sqlite3.Connection, table: str, owner_col: str
490	) -> dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]:
491	    """Resolve the many-to-many tag join for either ``TMTaskTag`` or
492	    ``TMAreaTag`` in one query, returning ``{owner_uuid: (name, ...)}``.
493	    """
494	    rows = con.execute(
495	        f"""
496	        SELECT j.{owner_col} AS owner, TMTag.title AS name
497	        FROM {table} AS j
498	        JOIN TMTag ON TMTag.uuid = j.tags
499	        """
500	    ).fetchall()
501	    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
502	    for r in rows:
503	        if r["name"]:
504	            out.setdefault(r["owner"], []).append(r["name"])
505	    return {k: tuple(v) for k, v in out.items()}
506	
507	
508	def _todo_from_row(r: sqlite3.Row, tags_by_task: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]) -> Todo:
509	    status = _STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN)
510	    stop = _from_unix(r["stopDate"])
511	    return Todo(
512	        id=r["uuid"],
513	        name=r["title"] or "",
514	        notes=r["notes"] or "",
515	        status=status,
516	        due_date=_decode_packed_date(r["deadline"]),
517	        activation_date=_decode_packed_date(r["startDate"]),
518	        completion_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_COMPLETED else None,
519	        cancellation_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_CANCELED else None,
520	        creation_date=_from_unix(r["creationDate"]),
521	        modification_date=_from_unix(r["userModificationDate"]),
522	        tag_names=tags_by_task.get(r["uuid"], ()),
523	        project_id=r["project"] or None,
524	        area_id=r["area"] or None,
525	        contact_id=r["contact"] or None,
526	        heading_id=r["heading"] or None,
527	        start_bucket=_start_bucket(r["start"]),
528	    )
529	
530	
531	def _project_from_row(r: sqlite3.Row, tags_by_task: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]) -> Project:
532	    status = _STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN)
533	    stop = _from_unix(r["stopDate"])
534	    return Project(
535	        id=r["uuid"],
536	        name=r["title"] or "",
537	        notes=r["notes"] or "",
538	        status=status,
539	        due_date=_decode_packed_date(r["deadline"]),
540	        activation_date=_decode_packed_date(r["startDate"]),
541	        completion_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_COMPLETED else None,
542	        cancellation_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_CANCELED else None,
543	        creation_date=_from_unix(r["creationDate"]),
544	        modification_date=_from_unix(r["userModificationDate"]),
545	        tag_names=tags_by_task.get(r["uuid"], ()),
546	        area_id=r["area"] or None,
547	    )
548

"""Read-only access to Things 3's on-disk SQLite database.

Things keeps its state in a WAL-mode SQLite file at::

    ~/Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/
        ThingsData-*/Things Database.thingsdatabase/main.sqlite

Enumerating entities through AppleScript is dominated by per-property IPC
roundtrips — a few hundred milliseconds per item once you read more than a
handful of fields. The same data read from SQLite comes back in
milliseconds regardless of count. For any read-heavy workload (sync
planning, reporting, bulk diffs) this is 100–1000× faster than
:class:`Things`.

Reads only. Writes must still go through :class:`Things` — poking Cultured
Code's private store directly would desynchronise Things' in-memory state
and its CloudKit sync. WAL mode makes concurrent reads safe while the app
is running.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import sqlite3
from contextlib import closing
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

from .models import Area, Contact, Heading, Project, StartBucket, Status, Tag, Todo


_DB_GLOB = (
    "Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/"
    "ThingsData-*/Things Database.thingsdatabase/main.sqlite"
)

# TMTask.type
_TYPE_TODO = 0
_TYPE_PROJECT = 1
_TYPE_HEADING = 2

# TMTask.status — the ints Things uses on disk, distinct from our Status enum.
_STATUS_BY_INT = {0: Status.OPEN, 2: Status.CANCELED, 3: Status.COMPLETED}
_STATUS_CANCELED = 2
_STATUS_COMPLETED = 3


def _default_db_path() -> Path:
    """Locate the Things 3 SQLite file in the current user's Library.

    Things writes to a container-ID directory that changes between
    installs, so we glob. Multiple ThingsData-* directories can exist if
    the app has been reinstalled; we take the most recently modified one.
    """
    candidates = list(Path.home().glob(_DB_GLOB))
    if not candidates:
        raise FileNotFoundError(
            "Things 3 database not found under ~/Library/Group Containers. "
            "Pass `path=` explicitly if your install is non-standard."
        )
    candidates.sort(key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
    return candidates[-1]


def _decode_packed_date(v: int | None) -> datetime | None:
    """Things packs `deadline` / `startDate` as a 32-bit int:
    year in bits 16+, month in 12–15, day in 7–11. 0 / NULL = unset.

    Returns a midnight datetime to match the AppleScript reader (which
    rounds through Things' own isoDate serializer with zeroed H/M/S).
    """
    if not v:
        return None
    y = v >> 16
    m = (v >> 12) & 0xF
    d = (v >> 7) & 0x1F
    if not (1 <= m <= 12 and 1 <= d <= 31 and y >= 1970):
        return None
    try:
        return datetime(y, m, d)
    except ValueError:
        return None


def _encode_packed_date(d) -> int:
    """Inverse of :func:`_decode_packed_date`. Accepts a date or datetime."""
    return (d.year << 16) | (d.month << 12) | (d.day << 7)


def _start_bucket(v: int | None) -> StartBucket:
    """Map TMTask.start (0/1/2) onto the StartBucket enum.

    Unexpected values fall through to ANYTIME — Things' internal schema has
    historically grown new codes, and leaving a foreign value unpinned would
    crash every reader the next time Cultured Code ships one.
    """
    try:
        return StartBucket(v)
    except ValueError:
        return StartBucket.ANYTIME


def _from_unix(v: float | None) -> datetime | None:
    if v is None:
        return None
    try:
        return datetime.fromtimestamp(v)
    except (OverflowError, OSError, ValueError):
        return None


class ThingsDB:
    """Read-only view onto Things' SQLite database.

    Mirrors the read surface of :class:`Things` (``todos``, ``projects``,
    ``areas``, ``tags``) but reads directly from disk. Returns the same
    dataclasses from :mod:`things_sync.models`, so call sites can swap
    between the two readers without downstream changes.

    Parameters
    ----------
    path
        Path to ``main.sqlite``. Autodetected in the default Things
        container if omitted.
    """

    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None) -> None:
        self.path = Path(path) if path else _default_db_path()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------ internals

    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
        # `mode=ro` opens read-only and never touches the file. We avoid
        # `immutable=1` because Things mutates the DB while we're reading.
        uri = f"file:{self.path}?mode=ro"
        con = sqlite3.connect(uri, uri=True)
        con.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
        return con

    # --------------------------------------------------------------- reads

    def todos(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
        """Every non-trashed todo (type=0), any status.

        Pass ``include_trashed=True`` to also return items in the Trash.
        Callers that only want active items should filter on
        ``status == Status.OPEN`` themselves — this method doesn't.
        """
        return self._tasks(_TYPE_TODO, include_trashed, _todo_from_row)

    def projects(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Project]:
        """Every non-trashed project (type=1), any status."""
        return self._tasks(_TYPE_PROJECT, include_trashed, _project_from_row)

    def headings(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Heading]:
        """Every non-trashed heading (type=2), any status.

        A heading lives inside a project; its ``project_id`` is the parent
        project's UUID. Todos under a heading reference it via their
        ``heading`` column (exposed on :class:`Todo` as ``heading_id``).
        """
        sql = """
            SELECT uuid, title, status, project
            FROM TMTask
            WHERE type = ?
        """
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
        sql += ' ORDER BY "index"'
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, (_TYPE_HEADING,)).fetchall()
        return [
            Heading(
                id=r["uuid"],
                name=r["title"] or "",
                project_id=r["project"] or None,
                status=_STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN),
            )
            for r in rows
        ]

    def areas(self) -> list[Area]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title FROM TMArea ORDER BY "index"'
            ).fetchall()
            tags_by_owner = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMAreaTag", "areas")
        return [
            Area(
                id=r["uuid"],
                name=r["title"] or "",
                tag_names=tags_by_owner.get(r["uuid"], ()),
                # TMArea doesn't persist the sidebar collapsed state in
                # the schema we know about; surface the default instead
                # of fabricating an unreliable value.
                collapsed=False,
            )
            for r in rows
        ]

    def tags(self) -> list[Tag]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag ORDER BY "index"'
            ).fetchall()
        return [
            Tag(
                id=r["uuid"],
                name=r["title"] or "",
                parent_id=r["parent"] or None,
                keyboard_shortcut=r["shortcut"] or "",
            )
            for r in rows
        ]

    def contacts(self) -> list[Contact]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, displayName FROM TMContact ORDER BY "index"'
            ).fetchall()
        return [Contact(id=r["uuid"], name=r["displayName"] or "") for r in rows]

    # --------------------------------------------------------- by-id lookups

    def todo(self, id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = True) -> Todo | None:
        return self._task_by_id(id, _TYPE_TODO, include_trashed, _todo_from_row)

    def project(self, id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = True) -> Project | None:
        return self._task_by_id(id, _TYPE_PROJECT, include_trashed, _project_from_row)

    def heading(self, id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = True) -> Heading | None:
        sql = "SELECT uuid, title, status, project FROM TMTask WHERE uuid=? AND type=?"
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND trashed=0"
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            r = con.execute(sql, (id, _TYPE_HEADING)).fetchone()
        if r is None:
            return None
        return Heading(
            id=r["uuid"],
            name=r["title"] or "",
            project_id=r["project"] or None,
            status=_STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN),
        )

    def area(self, id: str) -> Area | None:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            r = con.execute(
                "SELECT uuid, title FROM TMArea WHERE uuid=?", (id,)
            ).fetchone()
            tags_by_owner = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMAreaTag", "areas")
        if r is None:
            return None
        return Area(
            id=r["uuid"],
            name=r["title"] or "",
            tag_names=tags_by_owner.get(r["uuid"], ()),
            collapsed=False,
        )

    def tag(self, name: str) -> Tag | None:
        """Look up by display name (Things tags are name-unique)."""
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            r = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag WHERE title=?', (name,)
            ).fetchone()
        if r is None:
            return None
        return Tag(
            id=r["uuid"],
            name=r["title"] or "",
            parent_id=r["parent"] or None,
            keyboard_shortcut=r["shortcut"] or "",
        )

    def tag_by_id(self, id: str) -> Tag | None:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            r = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag WHERE uuid=?', (id,)
            ).fetchone()
        if r is None:
            return None
        return Tag(
            id=r["uuid"],
            name=r["title"] or "",
            parent_id=r["parent"] or None,
            keyboard_shortcut=r["shortcut"] or "",
        )

    # ------------------------------------------------------- filtered todos

    def todos_in_project(self, project_id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._tasks_filtered(_TYPE_TODO, "project=?", (project_id,), include_trashed)

    def todos_in_area(self, area_id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._tasks_filtered(_TYPE_TODO, "area=?", (area_id,), include_trashed)

    def todos_under_heading(self, heading_id: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._tasks_filtered(_TYPE_TODO, "heading=?", (heading_id,), include_trashed)

    def todos_in_list(self, name: str) -> list[Todo]:
        """Built-in virtual list — Things derives these from TMTask columns
        rather than storing them. The AppleScript ``to dos of list "X"``
        path is broken in Things 3.22.11 (returns -1728), so we
        re-implement the derivation against SQLite.

        Supported names (case-insensitive): Inbox, Today, Upcoming,
        Anytime, Someday, Logbook, Trash. Heuristic but matches the UI
        for the cases atlas cares about.
        """
        from datetime import date as _date

        n = name.lower()
        today = _encode_packed_date(_date.today())

        # base SELECT shared by all branches
        cols = (
            'uuid, title, notes, status, trashed, '
            'creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate, '
            'start, startDate, deadline, '
            'project, area, contact, heading'
        )
        if n == "trash":
            sql = f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask WHERE trashed=1 AND type=? ORDER BY "index"'
            params: tuple = (_TYPE_TODO,)
        elif n == "logbook":
            sql = (
                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status IN (2, 3) ORDER BY stopDate DESC'
            )
            params = (_TYPE_TODO,)
        elif n == "inbox":
            sql = (
                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 AND start=0 ORDER BY "index"'
            )
            params = (_TYPE_TODO,)
        elif n == "today":
            # Today = open + scheduled-and-arrived. Includes pinned via
            # todayIndexReferenceDate but leaving that for atlas to refine.
            sql = (
                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 '
                'AND startDate IS NOT NULL AND startDate <= ? '
                'ORDER BY todayIndex'
            )
            params = (_TYPE_TODO, today)
        elif n == "upcoming":
            sql = (
                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 '
                'AND startDate IS NOT NULL AND startDate > ? '
                'ORDER BY startDate, "index"'
            )
            params = (_TYPE_TODO, today)
        elif n == "anytime":
            # Active items in the Anytime bucket without a future schedule.
            sql = (
                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 AND start=1 '
                'AND (startDate IS NULL OR startDate <= ?) ORDER BY "index"'
            )
            params = (_TYPE_TODO, today)
        elif n == "someday":
            sql = (
                f'SELECT {cols} FROM TMTask '
                'WHERE type=? AND trashed=0 AND status=0 AND start=2 ORDER BY "index"'
            )
            params = (_TYPE_TODO,)
        else:
            raise ValueError(
                f"unknown built-in list {name!r}; expected one of "
                "Inbox, Today, Upcoming, Anytime, Someday, Logbook, Trash"
            )

        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]

    def todos_with_tag(self, name: str, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> list[Todo]:
        sql = """
            SELECT TMTask.uuid AS uuid, TMTask.title AS title, TMTask.notes AS notes,
                   TMTask.status AS status, TMTask.trashed AS trashed,
                   TMTask.creationDate, TMTask.userModificationDate, TMTask.stopDate,
                   TMTask.start, TMTask.startDate, TMTask.deadline,
                   TMTask.project, TMTask.area, TMTask.contact, TMTask.heading
            FROM TMTask
            JOIN TMTaskTag ON TMTaskTag.tasks = TMTask.uuid
            JOIN TMTag ON TMTag.uuid = TMTaskTag.tags
            WHERE TMTask.type = ? AND TMTag.title = ?
        """
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND TMTask.trashed = 0"
        sql += ' ORDER BY TMTask."index"'
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, (_TYPE_TODO, name)).fetchall()
            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]

    # --------------------------------------------------------- counts / exists

    def count_todos(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> int:
        return self._count(_TYPE_TODO, include_trashed)

    def count_projects(self, *, include_trashed: bool = False) -> int:
        return self._count(_TYPE_PROJECT, include_trashed)

    def count_areas(self) -> int:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            return con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMArea").fetchone()[0]

    def count_tags(self) -> int:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            return con.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMTag").fetchone()[0]

    def exists(self, id: str) -> bool:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            for sql, params in (
                ("SELECT 1 FROM TMTask WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1", (id,)),
                ("SELECT 1 FROM TMArea WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1", (id,)),
                ("SELECT 1 FROM TMTag WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1", (id,)),
            ):
                if con.execute(sql, params).fetchone() is not None:
                    return True
        return False

    # ---------------------------------------------------------- task helpers

    def _task_by_id(self, id: str, type_: int, include_trashed: bool, parse):
        sql = """
            SELECT uuid, title, notes, status, trashed,
                   creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate,
                   start, startDate, deadline,
                   project, area, contact, heading
            FROM TMTask
            WHERE uuid=? AND type=?
        """
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND trashed=0"
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            row = con.execute(sql, (id, type_)).fetchone()
            if row is None:
                return None
            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
        return parse(row, tags_by_task)

    def _tasks_filtered(self, type_: int, where: str, params: tuple, include_trashed: bool) -> list:
        sql = f"""
            SELECT uuid, title, notes, status, trashed,
                   creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate,
                   start, startDate, deadline,
                   project, area, contact, heading
            FROM TMTask
            WHERE type = ? AND {where}
        """
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
        sql += ' ORDER BY "index"'
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, (type_, *params)).fetchall()
            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]

    def _count(self, type_: int, include_trashed: bool) -> int:
        sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMTask WHERE type = ?"
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            return con.execute(sql, (type_,)).fetchone()[0]

    def _tasks(self, type_: int, include_trashed: bool, parse) -> list:
        sql = """
            SELECT uuid, title, notes, status, trashed,
                   creationDate, userModificationDate, stopDate,
                   start, startDate, deadline,
                   project, area, contact, heading
            FROM TMTask
            WHERE type = ?
        """
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += " AND trashed = 0"
        sql += ' ORDER BY "index"'
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, (type_,)).fetchall()
            tags_by_task = _tags_by_owner(con, "TMTaskTag", "tasks")
        return [parse(r, tags_by_task) for r in rows]


def _tags_by_owner(
    con: sqlite3.Connection, table: str, owner_col: str
) -> dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]:
    """Resolve the many-to-many tag join for either ``TMTaskTag`` or
    ``TMAreaTag`` in one query, returning ``{owner_uuid: (name, ...)}``.
    """
    rows = con.execute(
        f"""
        SELECT j.{owner_col} AS owner, TMTag.title AS name
        FROM {table} AS j
        JOIN TMTag ON TMTag.uuid = j.tags
        """
    ).fetchall()
    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for r in rows:
        if r["name"]:
            out.setdefault(r["owner"], []).append(r["name"])
    return {k: tuple(v) for k, v in out.items()}


def _todo_from_row(r: sqlite3.Row, tags_by_task: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]) -> Todo:
    status = _STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN)
    stop = _from_unix(r["stopDate"])
    return Todo(
        id=r["uuid"],
        name=r["title"] or "",
        notes=r["notes"] or "",
        status=status,
        due_date=_decode_packed_date(r["deadline"]),
        activation_date=_decode_packed_date(r["startDate"]),
        completion_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_COMPLETED else None,
        cancellation_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_CANCELED else None,
        creation_date=_from_unix(r["creationDate"]),
        modification_date=_from_unix(r["userModificationDate"]),
        tag_names=tags_by_task.get(r["uuid"], ()),
        project_id=r["project"] or None,
        area_id=r["area"] or None,
        contact_id=r["contact"] or None,
        heading_id=r["heading"] or None,
        start_bucket=_start_bucket(r["start"]),
    )


def _project_from_row(r: sqlite3.Row, tags_by_task: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]) -> Project:
    status = _STATUS_BY_INT.get(r["status"], Status.OPEN)
    stop = _from_unix(r["stopDate"])
    return Project(
        id=r["uuid"],
        name=r["title"] or "",
        notes=r["notes"] or "",
        status=status,
        due_date=_decode_packed_date(r["deadline"]),
        activation_date=_decode_packed_date(r["startDate"]),
        completion_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_COMPLETED else None,
        cancellation_date=stop if r["status"] == _STATUS_CANCELED else None,
        creation_date=_from_unix(r["creationDate"]),
        modification_date=_from_unix(r["userModificationDate"]),
        tag_names=tags_by_task.get(r["uuid"], ()),
        area_id=r["area"] or None,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum


class Status(str, Enum):
    OPEN = "open"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    CANCELED = "canceled"


class StartBucket(int, Enum):
    INBOX = 0
    ANYTIME = 1
    SOMEDAY = 2


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Todo:
    id: str
    name: str
    notes: str = ""
    status: Status = Status.OPEN
    due_date: datetime | None = None
    activation_date: datetime | None = None
    completion_date: datetime | None = None
    cancellation_date: datetime | None = None
    creation_date: datetime | None = None
    modification_date: datetime | None = None
    tag_names: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    project_id: str | None = None
    area_id: str | None = None
    contact_id: str | None = None
    heading_id: str | None = None
    start_bucket: StartBucket = StartBucket.ANYTIME


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Project:
    id: str
    name: str
    notes: str = ""
    status: Status = Status.OPEN
    due_date: datetime | None = None
    activation_date: datetime | None = None
    completion_date: datetime | None = None
    cancellation_date: datetime | None = None
    creation_date: datetime | None = None
    modification_date: datetime | None = None
    tag_names: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    area_id: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Area:
    id: str
    name: str
    tag_names: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    collapsed: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Heading:
    id: str
    name: str
    project_id: str | None = None
    status: Status = Status.OPEN


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Tag:
    id: str
    name: str
    parent_id: str | None = None
    keyboard_shortcut: str = ""


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Contact:
    id: str
    name: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ListInfo:
    id: str
    name: str

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from datetime import date, datetime
from enum import Enum


class Status(str, Enum):
    OPEN = "open"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    CANCELED = "canceled"


class StartBucket(int, Enum):
    """TMTask.start — which of Things' coarse buckets an item sits in.
    ANYTIME with a startDate is Today; SOMEDAY with a startDate is Upcoming."""

    INBOX = 0
    ANYTIME = 1
    SOMEDAY = 2


def _jsonable(v):
    if isinstance(v, Enum):
        return v.value
    if isinstance(v, (datetime, date)):
        return v.isoformat()
    if isinstance(v, tuple):
        return list(v)
    return v


def to_json_dict(obj) -> dict:
    return {k: _jsonable(v) for k, v in asdict(obj).items()}


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Todo:
    id: str
    title: str
    notes: str = ""
    status: Status = Status.OPEN
    start: StartBucket = StartBucket.ANYTIME
    when: date | None = None          # startDate — drives Today/Upcoming
    deadline: date | None = None
    evening: bool = False             # This Evening (only meaningful in Today)
    repeating: bool = False           # generated by a repeat rule
    trashed: bool = False
    project_id: str | None = None
    project_title: str | None = None
    area_id: str | None = None
    area_title: str | None = None
    heading_id: str | None = None
    heading_title: str | None = None
    tags: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    created: datetime | None = None
    modified: datetime | None = None
    stopped: datetime | None = None   # completion/cancellation time
    checklist_total: int = 0
    checklist_open: int = 0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Project:
    id: str
    title: str
    notes: str = ""
    status: Status = Status.OPEN
    when: date | None = None
    deadline: date | None = None
    trashed: bool = False
    area_id: str | None = None
    area_title: str | None = None
    tags: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    created: datetime | None = None
    modified: datetime | None = None
    stopped: datetime | None = None
    open_count: int = 0               # open, untrashed todos inside


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Heading:
    id: str
    title: str
    project_id: str | None = None
    project_title: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Area:
    id: str
    title: str
    visible: bool = True
    tags: tuple[str, ...] = ()


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Tag:
    id: str
    title: str
    shortcut: str = ""
    parent_id: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ChecklistItem:
    id: str
    title: str
    completed: bool = False
    stopped: datetime | None = None

input.content:
"""Read-only access to Things 3's on-disk SQLite database.

Things keeps its state in a WAL-mode SQLite file at::

    ~/Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/
        ThingsData-*/Things Database.thingsdatabase/main.sqlite

All reads go straight to that file (mode=ro) — milliseconds for the whole
database, no AppleScript IPC, and safe while the app runs thanks to WAL.
Writes never touch the file; they go through the Things URL scheme (see
urlscheme.py) so Things' in-memory state and CloudKit sync stay coherent.

List semantics follow what the Things UI actually shows (mirroring
things3-cli's battle-tested queries): repeating templates are hidden,
todos inside a trashed project/heading are hidden, Anytime includes
Today's items, and Today = confirmed + scheduled-and-arrived + overdue
deadlines.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import sqlite3
from contextlib import closing
from datetime import date, datetime
from pathlib import Path

from .models import (
    Area,
    ChecklistItem,
    Heading,
    Project,
    StartBucket,
    Status,
    Tag,
    Todo,
)

_DB_GLOB = (
    "Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/"
    "ThingsData-*/Things Database.thingsdatabase/main.sqlite"
)

# TMTask.type
_TYPE_TODO = 0
_TYPE_PROJECT = 1
_TYPE_HEADING = 2

# TMTask.status — on-disk ints, distinct from the Status enum.
_STATUS_INT = {Status.OPEN: 0, Status.CANCELED: 2, Status.COMPLETED: 3}
_STATUS_BY_INT = {0: Status.OPEN, 2: Status.CANCELED, 3: Status.COMPLETED}


class DBError(Exception):
    """A user-facing database problem (missing file, ambiguous id, …)."""


def default_db_path() -> Path:
    """Locate the Things 3 SQLite file: $THINGSDB, else glob the app group
    container (taking the most recently modified ThingsData-* dir)."""
    env = os.environ.get("THINGSDB")
    if env:
        return Path(env).expanduser()
    candidates = sorted(Path.home().glob(_DB_GLOB), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
    if not candidates:
        raise DBError(
            "Things 3 database not found under ~/Library/Group Containers "
            "(is Things installed?). Set THINGSDB to point at main.sqlite "
            "for a non-standard install."
        )
    return candidates[-1]


def decode_packed_date(v: int | None) -> date | None:
    """Things packs startDate/deadline as a 32-bit int: year in bits 16+,
    month in 12–15, day in 7–11. 0 / NULL = unset."""
    if not v:
        return None
    y, m, d = v >> 16, (v >> 12) & 0xF, (v >> 7) & 0x1F
    if not (1 <= m <= 12 and 1 <= d <= 31 and y >= 1970):
        return None
    try:
        return date(y, m, d)
    except ValueError:
        return None


def encode_packed_date(d: date) -> int:
    return (d.year << 16) | (d.month << 12) | (d.day << 7)


def _from_unix(v: float | None) -> datetime | None:
    if v is None:
        return None
    try:
        return datetime.fromtimestamp(v)
    except (OverflowError, OSError, ValueError):
        return None


def _start_bucket(v: int | None) -> StartBucket:
    # Unknown codes (Things grows new ones) fall through to ANYTIME rather
    # than crashing every reader.
    try:
        return StartBucket(v)
    except ValueError:
        return StartBucket.ANYTIME


# Shared SELECT for todos. project resolves through the heading too, so a
# todo filed under a heading still knows which project it belongs to.
_TODO_SELECT = """
    SELECT t.uuid, t.title, t.notes, t.status, t.trashed,
           t.start, t.startDate, t.startBucket, t.deadline,
           t.stopDate, t.creationDate, t.userModificationDate,
           (t.rt1_recurrenceRule IS NOT NULL OR t.repeater IS NOT NULL) AS repeating,
           t.checklistItemsCount, t.openChecklistItemsCount,
           COALESCE(t.project, h.project) AS project_id, p.title AS project_title,
           t.area AS area_id, a.title AS area_title,
           t.heading AS heading_id, h.title AS heading_title
    FROM TMTask t
    LEFT JOIN TMTask h ON h.uuid = t.heading
    LEFT JOIN TMTask p ON p.uuid = COALESCE(t.project, h.project)
    LEFT JOIN TMArea a ON a.uuid = t.area
    WHERE t.type = 0
"""

# Hide todos whose containing project/heading is in the Trash — they carry
# trashed=0 themselves but the UI doesn't show them anywhere.
_LIVE_CONTEXT = (
    " AND (p.uuid IS NULL OR p.trashed = 0)"
    " AND (h.uuid IS NULL OR h.trashed = 0)"
)
_NOT_TEMPLATE = " AND t.rt1_recurrenceRule IS NULL"

_TODAY_ORDER = (
    ' ORDER BY t.startBucket ASC, t.todayIndexReferenceDate DESC,'
    ' t.todayIndex ASC, t.uuid ASC'
)


class ThingsDB:
    """Read-only view onto Things' SQLite database."""

    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None) -> None:
        self.path = Path(path) if path else default_db_path()

    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
        # mode=ro never touches the file; no immutable=1 because Things
        # mutates the DB while we read.
        if not self.path.exists():
            raise DBError(f"Things database not found at {self.path}")
        con = sqlite3.connect(f"file:{self.path}?mode=ro", uri=True)
        con.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
        return con

    # ----------------------------------------------------------- built-in lists

    def inbox(self) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._todos('t.start = 0 AND t.status = 0 AND t.trashed = 0')

    def today(self) -> list[Todo]:
        """Today = confirmed (start=1 with a date) + scheduled-and-arrived
        (start=2, date <= today) + overdue deadlines with no schedule."""
        today = encode_packed_date(date.today())
        where = (
            "t.status = 0 AND t.trashed = 0 AND ("
            " (t.start = 1 AND t.startDate IS NOT NULL)"
            " OR (t.start = 2 AND t.startDate IS NOT NULL AND t.startDate <= ?)"
            " OR (t.startDate IS NULL AND t.deadline IS NOT NULL AND t.deadline <= ?"
            "     AND t.deadlineSuppressionDate IS NULL)"
            ")"
        )
        return self._todos(where, (today, today), order=_TODAY_ORDER)

    def upcoming(self) -> list[Todo]:
        today = encode_packed_date(date.today())
        return self._todos(
            't.status = 0 AND t.trashed = 0 AND t.start = 2'
            ' AND t.startDate IS NOT NULL AND t.startDate > ?',
            (today,),
            order=' ORDER BY t.startDate, t."index"',
        )

    def anytime(self) -> list[Todo]:
        # Matches the UI: Anytime includes items that are also in Today.
        return self._todos('t.start = 1 AND t.status = 0 AND t.trashed = 0')

    def someday(self) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._todos(
            't.start = 2 AND t.startDate IS NULL AND t.status = 0 AND t.trashed = 0'
        )

    def logbook(self, limit: int | None = None) -> list[Todo]:
        order = ' ORDER BY t.stopDate DESC'
        if limit is not None:
            order += f' LIMIT {int(limit)}'
        return self._todos('t.status IN (2, 3) AND t.trashed = 0', order=order)

    def trash(self) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._todos('t.trashed = 1', live_context=False)

    def deadlines(self) -> list[Todo]:
        return self._todos(
            't.deadline IS NOT NULL AND t.status = 0 AND t.trashed = 0',
            order=' ORDER BY t.deadline, t."index"',
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------------------- filtered

    def todos(
        self,
        *,
        status: Status | None = Status.OPEN,
        project_id: str | None = None,
        area_id: str | None = None,
        tag: str | None = None,
        query: str | None = None,
    ) -> list[Todo]:
        where, params = ['t.trashed = 0'], []
        if status is not None:
            where.append('t.status = ?')
            params.append(_STATUS_INT[status])
        if project_id is not None:
            where.append('COALESCE(t.project, h.project) = ?')
            params.append(project_id)
        if area_id is not None:
            # a todo belongs to an area directly or via its project
            where.append('(t.area = ? OR p.area = ?)')
            params.extend([area_id, area_id])
        if tag is not None:
            where.append(
                'EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TMTaskTag j JOIN TMTag g ON g.uuid = j.tags'
                ' WHERE j.tasks = t.uuid AND g.title = ?)'
            )
            params.append(tag)
        if query is not None:
            where.append('(t.title LIKE ? OR t.notes LIKE ?)')
            params.extend([f'%{query}%', f'%{query}%'])
        return self._todos(' AND '.join(where), tuple(params))

    def todo(self, id: str) -> Todo | None:
        rows = self._todos('t.uuid = ?', (id,), live_context=False, templates=True)
        return rows[0] if rows else None

    # ------------------------------------------------------ other entities

    def projects(
        self,
        *,
        area_id: str | None = None,
        status: Status | None = Status.OPEN,
        include_trashed: bool = False,
    ) -> list[Project]:
        sql = """
            SELECT t.uuid, t.title, t.notes, t.status, t.trashed,
                   t.startDate, t.deadline, t.stopDate,
                   t.creationDate, t.userModificationDate,
                   t.openUntrashedLeafActionsCount AS open_count,
                   t.area AS area_id, a.title AS area_title
            FROM TMTask t
            LEFT JOIN TMArea a ON a.uuid = t.area
            WHERE t.type = 1 AND t.rt1_recurrenceRule IS NULL
        """
        params: list = []
        if not include_trashed:
            sql += ' AND t.trashed = 0'
        if status is not None:
            sql += ' AND t.status = ?'
            params.append(_STATUS_INT[status])
        if area_id is not None:
            sql += ' AND t.area = ?'
            params.append(area_id)
        sql += ' ORDER BY t."index"'
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
            tags = _tags_by_owner(con, 'TMTaskTag', 'tasks')
        return [_project_from_row(r, tags) for r in rows]

    def project(self, id: str) -> Project | None:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            r = con.execute(
                """
                SELECT t.uuid, t.title, t.notes, t.status, t.trashed,
                       t.startDate, t.deadline, t.stopDate,
                       t.creationDate, t.userModificationDate,
                       t.openUntrashedLeafActionsCount AS open_count,
                       t.area AS area_id, a.title AS area_title
                FROM TMTask t LEFT JOIN TMArea a ON a.uuid = t.area
                WHERE t.type = 1 AND t.uuid = ?
                """,
                (id,),
            ).fetchone()
            tags = _tags_by_owner(con, 'TMTaskTag', 'tasks')
        return _project_from_row(r, tags) if r else None

    def headings(self, project_id: str) -> list[Heading]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, project FROM TMTask'
                ' WHERE type = 2 AND trashed = 0 AND project = ? ORDER BY "index"',
                (project_id,),
            ).fetchall()
        return [
            Heading(id=r['uuid'], title=r['title'] or '', project_id=r['project'])
            for r in rows
        ]

    def areas(self) -> list[Area]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, visible FROM TMArea ORDER BY "index"'
            ).fetchall()
            tags = _tags_by_owner(con, 'TMAreaTag', 'areas')
        return [
            Area(
                id=r['uuid'],
                title=r['title'] or '',
                visible=bool(r['visible']) if r['visible'] is not None else True,
                tags=tags.get(r['uuid'], ()),
            )
            for r in rows
        ]

    def area(self, id: str) -> Area | None:
        return next((a for a in self.areas() if a.id == id), None)

    def tags(self) -> list[Tag]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, shortcut, parent FROM TMTag ORDER BY "index"'
            ).fetchall()
        return [
            Tag(
                id=r['uuid'],
                title=r['title'] or '',
                shortcut=r['shortcut'] or '',
                parent_id=r['parent'],
            )
            for r in rows
        ]

    def checklist(self, todo_id: str) -> list[ChecklistItem]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(
                'SELECT uuid, title, status, stopDate FROM TMChecklistItem'
                ' WHERE task = ? ORDER BY "index"',
                (todo_id,),
            ).fetchall()
        return [
            ChecklistItem(
                id=r['uuid'],
                title=r['title'] or '',
                completed=r['status'] == 3,
                stopped=_from_unix(r['stopDate']),
            )
            for r in rows
        ]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------- resolution

    def resolve(self, ref: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
        """Resolve a full UUID or unique UUID prefix to ('todo' | 'project' |
        'heading' | 'area' | 'tag', full_uuid). Raises DBError if the ref is
        unknown or ambiguous."""
        kind_by_type = {_TYPE_TODO: 'todo', _TYPE_PROJECT: 'project', _TYPE_HEADING: 'heading'}
        matches: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            for exact in (True, False):
                cond = 'uuid = ?' if exact else 'substr(uuid, 1, ?) = ?'
                for sql, kind in (
                    (f'SELECT uuid, type FROM TMTask WHERE {cond}', None),
                    (f'SELECT uuid FROM TMArea WHERE {cond}', 'area'),
                    (f'SELECT uuid FROM TMTag WHERE {cond}', 'tag'),
                ):
                    params = (ref,) if exact else (len(ref), ref)
                    for r in con.execute(sql, params).fetchall():
                        k = kind or kind_by_type.get(r['type'], 'todo')
                        matches.append((k, r['uuid']))
                if matches:
                    break
        if not matches:
            raise DBError(f'no todo/project/area/tag matches id {ref!r}')
        if len(matches) > 1:
            ids = ', '.join(u[:12] + '…' for _, u in matches[:5])
            raise DBError(f'id prefix {ref!r} is ambiguous ({ids})')
        return matches[0]

    def project_by_name(self, name: str) -> Project | None:
        """Exact-title lookup among open, untrashed projects."""
        for p in self.projects(status=None):
            if p.title == name and p.status == Status.OPEN:
                return p
        return None

    def area_by_name(self, name: str) -> Area | None:
        return next((a for a in self.areas() if a.title == name), None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ misc

    def counts(self) -> dict[str, int]:
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            row = con.execute(
                """
                SELECT
                  SUM(type = 0 AND trashed = 0 AND status = 0
                      AND rt1_recurrenceRule IS NULL) AS open_todos,
                  SUM(type = 0 AND trashed = 0) AS todos,
                  SUM(type = 1 AND trashed = 0 AND status = 0) AS projects,
                  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMArea) AS areas,
                  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TMTag) AS tags
                FROM TMTask
                """
            ).fetchone()
        return {k: row[k] or 0 for k in row.keys()}

    def find_created_since(self, title: str, since: datetime) -> Todo | None:
        """Newest todo with this exact title created at/after `since` — used
        to recover the UUID right after an `add` fires through the URL
        scheme (which reports nothing back)."""
        rows = self._todos(
            't.title = ? AND t.creationDate >= ?',
            (title, since.timestamp()),
            order=' ORDER BY t.creationDate DESC',
            live_context=False,
        )
        return rows[0] if rows else None

    # -------------------------------------------------------------- internals

    def _todos(
        self,
        where: str,
        params: tuple = (),
        *,
        order: str = ' ORDER BY t."index"',
        live_context: bool = True,
        templates: bool = False,
    ) -> list[Todo]:
        sql = _TODO_SELECT + ' AND ' + where
        if live_context:
            sql += _LIVE_CONTEXT
        if not templates:
            sql += _NOT_TEMPLATE
        sql += order
        with closing(self._connect()) as con:
            rows = con.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
            tags = _tags_by_owner(con, 'TMTaskTag', 'tasks')
        return [_todo_from_row(r, tags) for r in rows]


def _tags_by_owner(
    con: sqlite3.Connection, table: str, owner_col: str
) -> dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]:
    rows = con.execute(
        f'SELECT j.{owner_col} AS owner, TMTag.title AS name'
        f' FROM {table} j JOIN TMTag ON TMTag.uuid = j.tags'
    ).fetchall()
    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for r in rows:
        if r['name']:
            out.setdefault(r['owner'], []).append(r['name'])
    return {k: tuple(v) for k, v in out.items()}


def _todo_from_row(r: sqlite3.Row, tags: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]) -> Todo:
    return Todo(
        id=r['uuid'],
        title=r['title'] or '',
        notes=r['notes'] or '',
        status=_STATUS_BY_INT.get(r['status'], Status.OPEN),
        start=_start_bucket(r['start']),
        when=decode_packed_date(r['startDate']),
        deadline=decode_packed_date(r['deadline']),
        evening=bool(r['startBucket']),
        repeating=bool(r['repeating']),
        trashed=bool(r['trashed']),
        project_id=r['project_id'],
        project_title=r['project_title'],
        area_id=r['area_id'],
        area_title=r['area_title'],
        heading_id=r['heading_id'],
        heading_title=r['heading_title'],
        tags=tags.get(r['uuid'], ()),
        created=_from_unix(r['creationDate']),
        modified=_from_unix(r['userModificationDate']),
        stopped=_from_unix(r['stopDate']),
        checklist_total=r['checklistItemsCount'] or 0,
        checklist_open=r['openChecklistItemsCount'] or 0,
    )


def _project_from_row(r: sqlite3.Row, tags: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]) -> Project:
    return Project(
        id=r['uuid'],
        title=r['title'] or '',
        notes=r['notes'] or '',
        status=_STATUS_BY_INT.get(r['status'], Status.OPEN),
        when=decode_packed_date(r['startDate']),
        deadline=decode_packed_date(r['deadline']),
        trashed=bool(r['trashed']),
        area_id=r['area_id'],
        area_title=r['area_title'],
        tags=tags.get(r['uuid'], ()),
        created=_from_unix(r['creationDate']),
        modified=_from_unix(r['userModificationDate']),
        stopped=_from_unix(r['stopDate']),
        open_count=r['open_count'] or 0,
    )
